title: #eng-releases: 6790012345
preview:
Alice Nguyen: Kicking off private on-prem rollout v3.4.0 to cluster oak-2 in the maintenance window now. FYI deploybot autosuppress is ON for scheduled backups.
DeployBot: [system] Canary slice started for v3.4.0 on oak-2. Metrics: p95=180ms -> 520ms, error-rate=0.8% (threshold 0.5%). Auto-rollback suppressed due to maintenance_flag=true (backup job overlap).
Ben Carter: Seeing 5xx spike from oak-2-app-1 in logs, correlation with backup window looks strong.
Carol Singh: If backup is causing IO contention we might be getting a false positive. Should we lift autosuppress and let the system rollback, or do a manual pin/rollback?
Dana Lopez: Oncall for oak-2 here — checking node metrics. Disk IO for node-3 at 95%, backup id bk-2026-03-22-1 running since 6790012000.
Eve Martinez: Recommend manual isolation: shift traffic off oak-2 app nodes and pin private deployment to v3.3.9 until morning. Safer than lifting autosuppress mid-backup.
Ben Carter: Pin command (if y'all agree): `redwood pin-version --deployment private --version v3.3.9 --scope oak-2`
Alice Nguyen: Running pin now and opening ops comms.
Alice Nguyen: ```
> redwood pin-version --deployment private --version v3.3.9 --scope oak-2
Output: OK — pinned v3.3.9 for scope oak-2, traffic shifted 65% -> 100% to pinned variant, rolling restarts scheduled.
```
DeployBot: Manual pin action recorded by alice in audit log (action_id: act-6790012350). Auto-rollback remains suppressed (maintenance flag).
Dana Lopez: Traffic shift completed. Current p95=165ms, error-rate=0.2%. Node IO dropping as backup finishes.
Carol Singh: Drafting a short release-note blurb for #release-notes: "Temporary rollback of private on-prem to v3.3.9 in EU (oak-2) during maintenance window due to maintenance-induced performance instability. No customer data impact. Investigating." Will post in 5.
Ben Carter: Filing incident for tracking: IR-20260322-1 with logs and backup window overlap callouts.
Alice Nguyen: Thanks team. Action items: (1) leave pinned until post-maintenance validation tomorrow morning, (2) eng-sre to investigate backup scheduling vs maintenance windows, (3) product comm to publish the blurb once Carol posts.
Eve Martinez: Follow-up suggestion: update DeployBot to include suppressed-reason metadata (backup job id + duration) in alerts so ops can correlate faster :+1:
oncall-bot: Incident IR-20260322-1 created and assigned to eng-sre (https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/IR-20260322-1).

title: #eng-releases: 6161616161
preview:
Alice Wang: heads up — noticed higher 95th latency on canary traffic after router patch. runtime still on v2.3.1, router bumped to v2.4.0 earlier today.
Ben Carter: ugh, which patch? the header-split one? :thinking_face: 
Alice Wang: yeah header-split + conn-pool tweak. console and api are on v2.3.x pinned by the rollout. router autoshift landed in prod.
Carol Nguyen: deploy-bot reported successful wave for router at 6161616100.
deploy-bot: wave completed: router v2.4.0 -> 100% (no errors). logs: /deploys/6161616100
Dan R.: how big is the 95th bump? numbers?
Alice Wang: from ~120ms -> ~210ms for tokenization-heavy requests. p50 unaffected.
Erin S.: could be header parsing change moving some work into runtime path due to header normalization. runtime now handles extra validation.
Ben Carter: we planned compatibility but forgot the runtime schema bump — oops. Is there a quick mitigation? pin router back, or roll runtime forward?
Alice Wang: runtime upgrade will take longer (model-serving infra). pinning router back is fastest.
Carol Nguyen: release notes for router include breaking change in header format. should've added a soft flag. I'll draft a note.
Dan R.: suggested rollback window? change window still open for another 45m.
Alice Wang: rollback router to v2.3.5 in next 10m unless anyone objects.
Ben Carter: +1
Erin S.: +1, also add a temporary routing rule to send canary % to runtime v2.3.1 only.
Carol Nguyen: I'll kick off pin and add a console banner and slack notify.
deploy-bot: starting rollback: router v2.4.0 -> v2.3.5 (initiated by carol nguyen)
Alice Wang: monitoring... p95 dropping back toward baseline.
Ben Carter: good catch. action items: 1) runtime team to prioritize schema fix; 2) router team to make header change behind feature flag; 3) we need clearer release notes checklist.
Carol Nguyen: I'll update release notes template to include 'compatibility matrix' and 'rollback runbook' checklist.
Erin S.: will open runtime PR for schema fix, target hotfix branch.
Dan R.: once runtime hotfix is validated we can re-roll router behind flag in next quiet window.
Alice Wang: thanks all. I'll annotate the deploy notes and create follow-up runbook in #eng-releases.
Carol Nguyen: posted banner and updated rollout notes. closing thread once metrics stable :thumbsup:

title: #docs: 4890109999
preview:
Leah Wong: FYI I opened a PR to reorganize the Rate Limits & Quotas guide into a single migration-friendly page and swapped multiple old snippets for the v2 SDK examples. https://github.com/redwoodinference/docs/pull/874
Owen Park: nice. can you add a short explicit example that shows which tier is charged when a request crosses reserved + burst? customers keep asking for a concrete numeric scenario.
Leah Wong: yep, planning a tiny worked example (3 lines) + one-line rule: reserved applies first, burst fills shortfalls up to cap, then pay-as-you-go.
Ben Carter: note for snippets: the new header is X-RW-Metering-Context. some examples still reference X-Metering or omit the header entirely. also v2 fluent API uses client.withQuota({tier: '...'}).
Leah Wong: good catch — I'll grep and replace across the page.
qa-bot: snippet-lint: found 2 instances of deprecated header X-Metering in examples/metering.md: lines 42, 97. Also inconsistent inline vs fenced code formatting.
Leah Wong: fixing both, will standardize to single backticks for inline flags and fenced blocks for full code.
Owen Park: on dashboards, the example panel image references metric metering.tiers_count but the query snippet uses metering.tier_counts_total — those should match or readers will copy the wrong query.
Leah Wong: thanks, aligning query and re-exporting panel PNG. I'll attach the new image to the PR.
Ben Carter: small migration note suggestion for Dedicated infra: "If your on-prem agent injects headers (agent <= v3.1), continue using X-Metering until you upgrade to agent v3.2+ which emits X-RW-Metering-Context." Keep it short.
Leah Wong: added under Migration -> Dedicated. linked agent release notes and added an internal pointer for solutions engineering.
Owen Park: can we add a one-line bullet clarifying embeddings billing? I.e., embeddings are billed per vector unit, not tokens. A customer pointed this out in support.
Leah Wong: added a parenthetical with a link to embeddings billing page.
docs-bot: PR 874 updated by leah - replaced headers, aligned metric names, updated panel screenshot. CI docs-lint: pass. snippet-check: failed (missing mock fixture).
Ben Carter: I'll push a tiny offline fixture that instantiates the v2 client and asserts the header and .withQuota() chain. No network required.
Ben Carter: pushed fixture -> branch. https://github.com/redwoodinference/docs/pull/874/files#diff-a1
docs-bot: snippet-check: pass after Ben's fixture. preview URL: https://deploy-preview-874--docs.redwood.ai/rate-limits-quotas
Owen Park: looks good in preview. one nit: use consistent casing for metric names in the prose (metering.tier_count -> metering.tier_count).
Leah Wong: fixed casing, ran linter again. merging after Netlify completes. I'll ping #support and #eng-runtime with the short migration blurb so folks expect the header rename.
Ben Carter: great, and I'll add the fixture to the docs-test-suite so future PRs don't regress the header example.
Leah Wong: thanks all! follow-ups: add a short runbook map for old->new metric names and note for Dedicated customers in the release checklist.

title: #eng-security: 4801234567
preview:
Alice Nguyen: Getting a spike of 403s on /scim/v2/Users deprovision for a handful of Okta tenants. Started ~09:12 UTC, saw ~50 failures in 5m. Anyone else?
Ben Carter: Saw it at 09:13. Correlates with last night's KMS alias swap. Quick grep shows JWE_DECRYPT_FAILED around that window.
Priya Singh: I'm pulling assertion logs and SCIM audit. Noting that some SAML assertions are missing the 'roles' attribute after yesterday's RBAC mapping change.
Samir Patel: App side shows JWE decrypt errors and then RBAC denies:
```
2026-03-21T09:12:45Z ERROR auth.jwt: JWE_DECRYPT_FAILED alias=redwood-kms-v2 key_id=ks_abc123 err=decryption failed
2026-03-21T09:12:45Z WARN scim.deprovision: deprov failed user=acct-42 customer=acme-corp status=403 request_id=req_789
```
Priya Singh: KMS audit shows decrypt calls returned OK for our service principal, but token verifier yields 403 because claim 'roles' is absent and the RBAC gate rejects.
Ben Carter: Can someone curl the SCIM endpoint with a freshly minted token from a repro session to confirm body?
Priya Singh: Repro token returns 403 and body: {"error":"forbidden","reason":"missing role:scim_deprov"}. The assertion contained groups but not the mapped 'roles' claim.
Alice Nguyen: We should enable full tracing for idp.assertion.* and scim.deprovision.* and alert infra. Also draft a customer note.
oncall-rotation: I'm oncall, escalating to infra for immediate alias rebind to previous key. Creating an incident.
idp-bot: Incident created: INC-2026-0042. Responders: @oncall-rotation, @Priya Singh, @Samir Patel.
Ben Carter: From vuln-triage view: no crypto exploit observed; likely timing/regression: RBAC attribute mapping change + KMS alias rotation overlapped.
Priya Singh: Short-term mitigation: add a feature-flagged fallback in token verifier to accept legacy claim shapes (e.g., look for 'groups' -> map to 'roles') for SCIM deprovision while we rollback/repair. Limit to 1 hour.
Samir Patel: I'll open a PR for the verifier fallback and a staging canary. If infra can rebind alias we can test quickly.
Alice Nguyen: Add structured audit events: scim.deprovision.fail and idp.assertion.missing_role with customer_id, request_id, token_kid, and affected_user. We'll need these for postmortem.
oncall-rotation: Assigning: Samir -> rollback PR + canary, Ben -> logs & correlation, Priya -> mapping fix + test plan, Alice -> customer comms and postmortem lead.
Ben Carter: Pushed a log-collector change to staging to count missing-role occurrences by tenant.
Priya Singh: After infra rollback and RBAC mapping revert, confirm deprovision succeeds for acme-corp + two other tenants.
Samir Patel: Rollback PR merged to staging, deploying now. Patch is behind a feature flag; canary in 10m.
Alice Nguyen: Root cause hypothesis: RBAC attribute mapping change removed a required claim at the same time a KMS alias rotation introduced a transient key-binding mismatch. Together caused a wave of 403s.
Priya Singh: Action items: immediate alias rollback, revert mapping, add verifier fallback, add monitoring for missing role claims, update runbook to prevent alias rotations during provisioning windows, and add a postmortem. I'll also start a vuln-triage note to verify no security regression introduced by the fallback.
oncall-rotation: I'll send a brief advisory to affected customers and update the incident timeline. Thanks all. :thumbsup:
Alice Nguyen: Good catch everyone. Keep updates in this thread and paste the final timeline into the postmortem doc.
Priya Singh: Note for postmortem: include exact timestamps for alias rotation, RBAC rollout, and first 403s; include affected tenant list and counts.
Samir Patel: Will attach canary logs and k8s rollout IDs once done.
Ben Carter: Collecting logs and will upload to INC-2026-0042.
Alice Nguyen: Closing loop: once rollback stable, schedule a follow-up meeting to discuss safer rollout gating.
Priya Singh: Marking follow-up: RBAC mapping gate + KMS alias post-checks.
oncall-rotation: Incident state -> investigating -> mitigated (pending postmortem).

title: #eng-security: 4791234567
preview:
Alice Nguyen: Heads up — getting a spike of 403s on /scim/v2/Users deprovision for a few Okta customers. Started ~09:12 UTC. Anyone else seeing this?
Ben Carter: Yep, saw it ~09:13. Correlates with last night's KMS alias swap.

2026-03-21T09:12:45Z WARN scim.deprovision: deprov failed user=acct-42 customer=acme-corp status=403
```

```bash

title: #architecture: 4623512345
preview:
Alice Nguyen: Heads up — thinking about consolidating the current "mux" routing adapters into a single multiplexing abstraction. Idea: unify shard-aware, cost-aware, and geo-routing under one minimal API so we can add new heuristics without adapter explosion.

Ben Carter: +1 on consolidation. Can you sketch what the abstraction looks like? worried about performance and lock contention.

Alice Nguyen: rough sketch:
```
mux.Resolve(request) -> [RouteCandidate{id, score, metadata}]
mux.CacheHint(key) -> prefetch/kvcache keys
```
Edge vs coordinator separation: edge does quick heuristic; coordinator runs heavier scoring.

Carla M: @Alice what's the fallback story? we still need per-tenant overrides and canary policy.

Alice Nguyen: Plan:
- minimal runtime API (Resolve/Score/Explain)
- plugin layer for tenant overrides (sandboxed)
- compat-shim for old adapters
- rollout via header: X-Routing-Mode: mux_v1 or legacy

Dmitri Ivanov: How do we avoid double-counting cost/latency metrics? telemetry needs a "decision_event" with inputs and final score. also keep sampling low overhead.

Eve Li: Agree. Also consider moving heavy scoring to async background re-ranker so latency SLOs maintained.

Ben Carter: Implementation concerns:
- batching of Resolve calls to reduce DB/redis lookups
- kernel: in-memory candidate pool per region
- prefer no global locks; use compare-and-swap for lease updates

infra-bot: perf CI for mux branch: all tests passed (mux/*) — run 45m, p95 latency increase 0.8% :eyes:

Carla M: About plugin layer — sandboxing options?
- WASM plugin (safe, fast)
- Lua in-process (fast but riskier)
- remote policy service (language-agnostic but network bound)

Alice Nguyen: leaning WASM for safety. Will start prototype: rust -> wasm. Need eval harness.

Ben Carter: For compatibility, let's keep adapter facade:
```
if header == "legacy": adapter.LegacyResolve(req)
else: mux.Resolve(req)
```
and add feature flag to flip per-tenant.

Dmitri Ivanov: Two risky things: 1) telemetry explosion (we'll need to limit explain traces), 2) rollout speed: we must be able to kill mux path quickly.

Eve Li: Propose kill-switch via control-plane flag + fallback to legacy with zero-impact on req path.

Carla M: Also require perf gating: mux rollout only if qps bench < 5% regress, mem delta < 100MB/pod.

Alice Nguyen: Work items (first cut):
1) proto for Resolve/Score/Explain (PR)
2) WASM plugin harness + sample tenant policy
3) compat-shim + feature flag path
4) perf harness + CI gating
5) telemetry schema + sample size cap

Ben Carter: timeline? Can we get an initial prototype in 3 weeks? :timer_clock:

Alice Nguyen: 3 weeks for proto + WASM POC sounds doable. Will add ticket in RFC board. I'll @Ben and @Carla for pre-review.

Carla M: :thumbsup: Add a test that simulates regional failover + constrained capacity.

Dmitri Ivanov: We'll need to update console to show mux decisions (explain) — add to roadmap.

Alice Nguyen: noted. I'll post a short followup RFC doc with config examples and schemas.

infra-bot: deploy-bot: canary mux-v1 rolled to 10% tenants at 2026-03-21T15:22Z

Maria Chen: Heads-up — RC cut is tomorrow 09:00 PST. We need coordinated version bumps across api, runtime, console, and SDKs. Please drop PR links + 1–2 changelog bullets here. Any blockers?

Ajay Patel: Quick Q — are we pinning runtime to v4.1.0 or doing v4.1.1-rc for this cut?

Sophia Ruiz: I'll take api + console. API PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/api/pull/1876 (bump v1.12.0)
Console PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/console/pull/542 (bump v2.4.0)
Changelog bullets: "fix: normalize auth header parsing", "chore: surface token-level latency in route table"

release-bot: Deploy window scheduled: 2026-03-19 09:00 PST → 09:30 PST. Branch freeze at 2026-03-19 08:30 PST. Please add label `ready-for-cut` when merged.

Ben Carter: Changelog PR ready: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/333 — wording suggestions? leaning toward "fix: auth header normalization" (keeps scope narrow).

Olivia Park: SDKs: PR for JS client: https://github.com/redwood-inference/sdk-js/pull/210 (bump v0.9.1) — note: default timeout changed (10000 -> 15000ms) which is a mild break for folks relying on older default. I'll add migration note in the changelog.

Nathan Li: Runtime: holding off on the new quant kernel landing until after cut. Runtime PR (compat fixes + bump): https://github.com/redwood-inference/runtime/pull/998 — ran local compat tests: 120/123 passed, failing on softmax precision in longseq mode. Investigating, but should be fixable before 06:30 PST.

Ajay Patel: Console e2e — can someone confirm staging runs? I don't want a regression in rollout UI.

Sophia Ruiz: Console e2e passed on staging at 02:33 PST (run #712). No regressions observed on the route UI or rollout modal.

Maria Chen: Action items: @Sophia — merge API + console PRs by 05:00 PST. @Olivia — merge SDKs by 06:00 PST (include migration note). @Nathan — rebase runtime, push a compat fix and rerun tests by 06:30 PST. @Ben — finalize changelog wording and link to merged PRs. Ping me when each is merged. :thumbsup:

release-bot: Label `ready-for-cut` added to: https://github.com/redwood-inference/api/pull/1876, https://github.com/redwood-inference/console/pull/542, https://github.com/redwood-inference/sdk-js/pull/210

Ajay Patel: I'll do the final branch pin and tag at 08:45 PST unless someone requests an earlier hold. Command I'll run (for visibility):

```
git checkout release
git merge --no-ff --no-edit origin/main
git tag -a v1.12.0 -m "release: v1.12.0 RC"
git push origin release --tags
```

Maria Chen: Perfect. If runtime tests still flake, we can drop runtime into the first-stage fallback (dedicated v4.1.0) and ship API/console/SDKs. We want to avoid blocking everything for a single kernel tweak.

Nathan Li: Acknowledged — I'll prioritize the softmax precision fix and post a quick patch PR by 05:40 PST if possible. If not, I'll flag runtime for fallback.

Sophia Ruiz: Noted — merging API + console now after final QA ✅

Ben Carter: Updated changelog wording and linked merged PRs: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/333 — ready for final review.

Olivia Park: SDK merged and migration note added. https://github.com/redwood-inference/sdk-js/pull/210 — tagging release lead.

Ajay Patel: I'll post back once I've pinned and pushed the tag (08:45 PST).

Maria Chen: Closing loop — keep updates short here. Good luck!"

title: #docs: 3967890123
preview:
Lena Ortiz: Opening a small docs PR to clarify microbilling rounding and show SDK cost examples. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/642
Ben Carter: Important — customers confused by fractional cents. Add a short explainer: we calculate cost = ceil(tokens/1000 * price_per_1k * 100)/100 (round up to cents) unless customer opts into exact microbilling.
Sofia Alvarez: Could we include both behaviors? show the default rounding-to-cents and an opt-in header `X-Exact-Microbill: true` for advanced accounts.
Lena Ortiz: Draft Python snippet for default rounding:
```python
tokens = 125
price_per_1k = 0.0025
cost = ((tokens/1000)*price_per_1k)
# round up to cents by default
cost_cents = math.ceil(cost*100)/100
print(f"Estimated cost: ${cost_cents:.2f}")
```
Markus Voss: Add a TS snippet too; keep it very small. Also call out that SDKs will expose `response.headers.get('X-Usage-Cost')` once microbilling is calculated server-side.
Lena Ortiz: TS snippet:
```ts
const tokens = 125;
const pricePer1k = 0.0025;
const raw = (tokens/1000)*pricePer1k;
const cost = Math.ceil(raw*100)/100; // default
console.log(`$${cost.toFixed(2)}`);
```
Ben Carter: For the docs page, add a short table comparing: default cents rounding vs exact microbilling, with examples for 1 token, 125 tokens, 15300 tokens. PMs want that for pricing FAQ.
Sofia Alvarez: Console update: the Token consumption panel should show both "Tokens" and a new column "Estimated cost (rounded)" for quick preview. Ops will need to add the column mapping. Markus can coordinate with them.
Markus Voss: I'll open a small console PR to add the column and send the grafana link to the docs PR. Lena, include a note in migration section: older Dedicated proxies might not send `X-Usage-Cost` — fallback is compute client-side using published price per model.
Lena Ortiz: Added migration note draft and a troubleshooting bullet for when `X-Usage-Cost` missing. Also added a tiny curl example showing headers:
```bash
curl -i -H "Authorization: Bearer $REDWOOD_KEY" https://api.redwood.ai/v2/generate | grep -i "X-Usage-Cost" || echo "header missing"
```
DocsBot: PR 642 opened by Lena Ortiz — CI: docs-build (staging) passed ✅ — Preview: https://deploy-preview-642--docs.redwood.ai
Ben Carter: QA checklist: verify math examples with finance, update pricing FAQ copy, add changelog line `docs: clarify microbilling rounding and SDK examples`, and tag i18n for the new Console column label.
Lena Ortiz: Thanks everyone. Aiming to merge Fri AM. Will ping #announcements when live. :white_check_mark:

title: #incidents: 3905553333
preview:
oncall-bot: ALERT: High NXDOMAIN rate detected for api.redwood-inference.com from EU resolver pool (eu-west-*). Severity: P1. Trigger: dns-monitoring rule dns.nxdomain.rate > 5% for 3m. Link: https://ops.redwood/internal/incidents/3905553333
oncall-bot: Incident channel auto-created: #inc-3905553333 (paging SRE oncall)
Erin Nguyen: acked. I'm on the line and pulling graphs. Seeing spike starting ~3905553200 (approx 8m ago).
Maya Patel: looking at resolver probes now. Quick dig from public resolvers shows NXDOMAIN from some IPs, SERVFAIL from others. Copying samples:
```
;; Querying 1.1.1.1
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, status: NXDOMAIN, id: 12345
;; flags: qr rd ra; QUERY: 1, ANSWER: 0
;; QUESTION SECTION:
;api.redwood-inference.com. IN A
```
```
;; Querying 34.216.34.22 (eu resolver)
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, status: SERVFAIL, id: 67890
```
Maya Patel: early hypothesis: partial zone push to authoritative cluster caused inconsistent serials between authoritative name servers -> some NSs answering NXDOMAIN while others fine. Sounds like a race during zone reload.
Jordan Kline: confirmed from our side — pushing zones to provider-auth1 and provider-auth2. provider-auth1 rolled successfully, provider-auth2 got a transient failure during transfer and rolled back to empty zone for a few minutes (we see serial 0 on some servers). This leads to NXDOMAIN for delegated records.
Ben Carter: customer impact: internal telemetry shows ~7% of API hostname lookups from EU edge failing with NXDOMAIN -> causes client errors and SAML auth flips for EU customers. Support flagged 12 tickets so far.
Maya Patel: mitigation plan: 1) stop further zone updates, 2) roll back to last known-good serial on provider-auth2, 3) temporarily lower TTLs won't help here (they control caching) but we can ask edge cache to respect stale-while-revalidate. Also prepare CNAME fallback for the most critical subdomain.
Jordan Kline: executing emergency rollback to serial 2026040103 on provider-auth2 via API now. Command used:
```
curl -X POST https://api.authoritative-provider.example.com/zones/zoneid/rollback \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer $PROVIDER_TOKEN' \
  -d '{"serial":2026040103}'
```
Jordan Kline: rollback accepted by provider API, propagation in progress. Notifying Cloudflare/Route53 federated cache to refresh via purge endpoints.
oncall-bot: deploy-bot: zone rollback job started (job id 98321).
Lucy Zhao: good. Opened a provider support ticket and requested a contact escalation. Ticket 98765. Mentioned urgent business impact and asked for confirmation of which NSs served empty zone during the window.
Maya Patel: metrics check 5m after rollback: NXDOMAIN rate down from 18% to 3% in EU. Some resolvers still returning cached NXDOMAIN; TTL and negative caching make this noisy. Proposing temporary traffic mitigation: adjust GeoDNS routing to prefer secondaries that served correct zone earlier, and add short-lived CNAME fallback for auth.example endpoints to fallback-host.internal.redwood.
Ben Carter: support comms: drafting banner and outbound note. Core content: impacted regions (EU), affected endpoints (api.redwood-inference.com and auth.redwood-inference.com), mitigation taken (rollback to known good zone, contacting providers), ETA for full resolution: monitor 30-60m for negative-cache expiry.
Maya Patel: additional technical note: negative caching (SOA negative TTL / NXTTL) was 3600s on the bad serial; provider is lowering negative TTL to 60s for emergency to speed recovery. They confirmed the change.
Erin Nguyen: thanks. Can we also enable 'serve-stale' on our DNS resolvers so edge returns last good answer for a short period while upstream heals? That will reduce customer impact for read-heavy paths.
Jordan Kline: serve-stale enabled on our resolvers (config push id 7742). Monitoring.
Leo Ramos: I'm the next oncall, will take handoff in 15m. Leaving runbook notes: rollback executed, provider ticket 98765, purge requests sent, serve-stale enabled, monitoring dashboards updated. Will watch for recurrence on zone pushes.
Maya Patel: creating follow-up action items for postmortem: 1) provider transfer retry logic - make rollbacks atomic, 2) test harness for zone push under load, 3) update runbook to include provider-side negative TTL adjustments, 4) ensure alerts fire on serial mismatch across NS set.
Support Bot: Support draft prepared and pinned in channel for review.
Ben Carter: marking comms ready for send after Lucy review. Lucy, can you review and ok?
Lucy Zhao: reviewing now, will sign off in 5m.
Erin Nguyen: immediate status: incident now in monitoring (P1 -> monitoring). NXDOMAIN rate trending down. Will keep channel open for 60m then update status to resolved if no regression.
Leo Ramos: ack, I'll escalate immediately if rate rises above 5% again. Handing off on-call to Leo now (coming up). :handshake:
Maya Patel: thanks all — adding postmortem owner tag to Leo.

text:
oncall-bot: ALERT: High NXDOMAIN rate detected for api.redwood-inference.com from EU resolver pool (eu-west-*). Severity: P1. Trigger: dns-monitoring rule dns.nxdomain.rate > 5% for 3m. Link: https://ops.redwood/internal/incidents/3905553333
oncall-bot: Incident channel auto-created: #inc-3905553333 (paging SRE oncall)
Erin Nguyen: acked. I'm on the line and pulling graphs. Seeing spike starting ~3905553200 (approx 8m ago).
Maya Patel: looking at resolver probes now. Quick dig from public resolvers shows NXDOMAIN from some IPs, SERVFAIL from others. Copying samples:
```
;; Querying 1.1.1.1
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, status: NXDOMAIN, id: 12345
;; flags: qr rd ra; QUERY: 1, ANSWER: 0
;; QUESTION SECTION:
;api.redwood-inference.com. IN A
```
```
;; Querying 34.216.34.22 (eu resolver)
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, status: SERVFAIL, id: 67890
```
Maya Patel: early hypothesis: partial zone push to authoritative cluster caused inconsistent serials between authoritative name servers -> some NSs answering NXDOMAIN while others fine. Sounds like a race during zone reload.
Jordan Kline: confirmed from our side — pushing zones to provider-auth1 and provider-auth2. provider-auth1 rolled successfully, provider-auth2 got a transient failure during transfer and rolled back to empty zone for a few minutes (we see serial 0 on some servers). This leads to NXDOMAIN for delegated records.
Ben Carter: customer impact: internal telemetry shows ~7% of API hostname lookups from EU edge failing with NXDOMAIN -> causes client errors and SAML auth flips for EU customers. Support flagged 12 tickets so far.
Maya Patel: mitigation plan: 1) stop further zone updates, 2) roll back to last known-good serial on provider-auth2, 3) temporarily lower TTLs won't help here (they control caching) but we can ask edge cache to respect stale-while-revalidate. Also prepare CNAME fallback for the most critical subdomain.
Jordan Kline: executing emergency rollback to serial 2026040103 on provider-auth2 via API now. Command used:
```
curl -X POST https://api.authoritative-provider.example.com/zones/zoneid/rollback \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer $PROVIDER_TOKEN' \
  -d '{"serial":2026040103}'
```
Jordan Kline: rollback accepted by provider API, propagation in progress. Notifying Cloudflare/Route53 federated cache to refresh via purge endpoints.
oncall-bot: deploy-bot: zone rollback job started (job id 98321).
Lucy Zhao: good. Opened a provider support ticket and requested a contact escalation. Ticket 98765. Mentioned urgent business impact and asked for confirmation of which NSs served empty zone during the window.
Maya Patel: metrics check 5m after rollback: NXDOMAIN rate down from 18% to 3% in EU. Some resolvers still returning cached NXDOMAIN; TTL and negative caching make this noisy. Proposing temporary traffic mitigation: adjust GeoDNS routing to prefer secondaries that served correct zone earlier, and add short-lived CNAME fallback for auth.example endpoints to fallback-host.internal.redwood.
Ben Carter: support comms: drafting banner and outbound note. Core content: impacted regions (EU), affected endpoints (api.redwood-inference.com and auth.redwood-inference.com), mitigation taken (rollback to known good zone, contacting providers), ETA for full resolution: monitor 30-60m for negative-cache expiry.
Maya Patel: additional technical note: negative caching (SOA negative TTL / NXTTL) was 3600s on the bad serial; provider is lowering negative TTL to 60s for emergency to speed recovery. They confirmed the change.
Erin Nguyen: thanks. Can we also enable 'serve-stale' on our DNS resolvers so edge returns last good answer for a short period while upstream heals? That will reduce customer impact for read-heavy paths.
Jordan Kline: serve-stale enabled on our resolvers (config push id 7742). Monitoring.
Leo Ramos: I'm the next oncall, will take handoff in 15m. Leaving runbook notes: rollback executed, provider ticket 98765, purge requests sent, serve-stale enabled, monitoring dashboards updated. Will watch for recurrence on zone pushes.
Maya Patel: creating follow-up action items for postmortem: 1) provider transfer retry logic - make rollbacks atomic, 2) test harness for zone push under load, 3) update runbook to include provider-side negative TTL adjustments, 4) ensure alerts fire on serial mismatch across NS set.
Support Bot: Support draft prepared and pinned in channel for review.
Ben Carter: marking comms ready for send after Lucy review. Lucy, can you review and ok?
Lucy Zhao: reviewing now, will sign off in 5m.
Erin Nguyen: immediate status: incident now in monitoring (P1 -> monitoring). NXDOMAIN rate trending down. Will keep channel open for 60m then update status to resolved if no regression.
Leo Ramos: ack, I'll escalate immediately if rate rises above 5% again. Handing off on-call to Leo now (coming up). :handshake:
Maya Patel: thanks all — adding postmortem owner tag to Leo.

title: #incidents: 3380012345
preview:
OpsBot: ALERT: ACME Corp (tenant: acme-prod-vpc) - automated eval detected a quality regression for model `redwood-v2-quant-8bit` on dedicated pool. Relative score drop: ~18% on invoice_e2e eval. Triggered 2026-03-15T10:12Z. Paging on-call.
Diego Ramos: oncall here, seeing the page. I'll pull logs and current quant profile.
Diego Ramos: @Mira Chen can you confirm if any recent rollout touched tenant-specific quant profiles?
Mira Chen: I didn't push any tenant-specific changes today. We did a global push of a new quant kernel at 09:50 but it should be opt-in per-tenant; double-checking.
Tariq Patel: I'm looking at the dedicated pool for acme-prod-vpc. Host nodes show recent restart around 09:53, but pods came back green. No infra resource pressure spikes.
Ben Carter: Customer CS: ACME reported a spike in "nonsense" tokens in generated invoices at 10:18 UTC. They pegged it to our responses being truncated/incorrect line items. Impacting production.
Mira Chen: Ok — likely a mismatched quant profile or kernel path being used for that tenant. Plan options: (1) revert tenant quant profile to previous working profile; (2) route ACME to hosted float16 model as temporary fallback; (3) short-term rate-limit to reduce noise while we validate. Thoughts?
Alex Rivera: priority 1 is restore quality for customer. Revert tenant profile first. If that fails, route to hosted variant. Need comms to ACME either way.
Diego Ramos: I'll try revert first. Sharing commands I'll run now:```
# revert quant profile for tenant
curl -X POST "https://controlplane.redwood.internal/tenants/acme-prod-vpc/quant_profiles/revert" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $REVERT_TOKEN" \
  -d '{"target_version":"profile-1.2"}'
# restart routing for tenant service
kubectl -n dedicated-acme rollout restart deployment/acme-serving
```
Diego Ramos: running revert now. ETA ~3-4m.
OpsBot: ACTION: Handoff note added to incident log. Severity: Customer-Impact (P1).
Diego Ramos: revert request succeeded (HTTP 202). Restarting deployment...
Diego Ramos: restart complete, doing quick smoke eval against invoice_e2e on canary shard.
Mira Chen: I'll run a small eval with the previous golden prompt set and compare token-level metrics.
Mira Chen: eval result: quality metrics back to baseline within noise (~+1% vs pre-regression).
Ben Carter: ACME on phone — they confirm responses look normal now. Thanks. They're asking for a timeline and root cause.
Tariq Patel: infra logs show the dedicated pool picked up the new global quant kernel at 09:50 but our per-tenant selector rule failed to prefer the tenant's pinned profile because of a config race during node reboot. In short: kernel advertised globally before tenant config reconciled.
Alex Rivera: good find. Immediate mitigation = confirmed revert executed and canary eval green. Next: patch reconciliation order and add an extra check before global kernel advertisement.
Katie Liu: I'll draft the customer note: scope, timeline, mitigation, and next steps. Ben, want me to send to ACME directly or you handle?
Ben Carter: please send — include that we reverted tenant quant profile to profile-1.2, mitigation window 10:12-10:46 UTC, and that we will follow up with a postmortem and permanent fix.
Diego Ramos: adding runbook entry for tenant-specific rollback steps. Snippet: ```
# rollback runbook excerpt
1) POST /tenants/<tenant>/quant_profiles/revert {target_version}
2) kubectl -n dedicated-<tenant> rollout restart deployment/<serving>
3) run eval: curl http://eval-service/score?set=invoice_e2e&model=<model>
4) confirm metrics within SLA
```
Mira Chen: also adding automated alert to detect when a global kernel advertisement happens within 5m of node restarts for a tenant pool.
OpsBot: NOTE: Oncall Handoff planned at 11:00 UTC. Diego -> Mira for follow-up and postmortem.
Diego Ramos: handing off to @Mira Chen for postmortem. I've attached logs and the revert request id: revert-20260315-ACME-01.
Mira Chen: acknowledged, taking lead on postmortem. I'll coordinate with Tariq for infra timeline and Alex for product action items.
Ben Carter: customer notified at 10:55 UTC. They appreciated the quick fix.
Alex Rivera: action items assigned: Mira (postmortem + reconciliation change), Tariq (infra gating + alerts), Diego (document rollback runbook), Katie (customer comms).
Mira Chen: initial postmortem draft incoming within 24h. Will include root cause, impact window, and verification artifacts.
OpsBot: INCIDENT STATUS: mitigated. Summary: tenant quant profile reverted -> quality restored. Customer impact window 10:12 - 10:46 UTC. Postmortem required.
Diego Ramos: closing my oncall loop. Ping me if anything regresses.
Mira Chen: thanks team.
Katie Liu: sent ACME note, included rollback details and next steps.
Ben Carter: ACME replied with :thumbsup:.
Mira Chen: last check — automated eval after 30m shows stable metrics. Closing thread once PM review complete.

title: #incidents: 3337778888
preview:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: ALERT: High GPU OOM rate on decode/stream pool for route /v1/generate. Request drops increasing. Metric: decode_gpu_ooms=18/min, dropped_requests=7%. Paging on-call.
Alice Nguyen: I'm on it. quick summary? which route + customers impacted?
incident-bot: Route: /v1/generate. Cluster: decode-prod-us-west. First spike @3337778860. Affected model: rdx-large-decoder-v2 and its quantized fallback.
Samir Patel: seeing repeated `max_tokens=null` in incoming requests for a subset of traffic — looks like SDK edge-case not setting token cap. decode loop keeps allocating KV cache until CUDA OOM.
Javier Morales: logs attached (excerpt):
```
E 2025-11-02T18:21:43Z CudaError: out of memory at decode.cu:421
GPU 0: 11.9 GiB used of 12.0 GiB
Trace: decode_step -> attention_kv_alloc -> cudaMalloc failed
```
Javier Morales: this is triggering container restarts on 4 pods; 2 nodes have flapped in the last 12m. :fire:
Priya Kaur: immediate mitigation options? Can we enforce a hard token cap at the routing layer and route offenders to a safe model?
Samir Patel: yes. plan: 1) enforce route-level max_tokens=512 2) enable fallback to rdx-large-quantized 3) throttle burst concurrency for route 4) cordon/drain flapping nodes.
Samir Patel: applying route cap now. command: 
```
redwoodctl policy update --route /v1/generate --set max_tokens=512 --enforce=true
```
Maya Chen: support: 12 open user tickets referencing 503/empty responses. 3 customers report production impact (one >50% errors). Escalating CS priority.
Ben Carter: notified the larger customer (AcmeCorp) and opened a bridge. They're seeing ~60% failures on streaming calls.
Samir Patel: also enabling quick fallback policy: route will try rdx-large-decoder-v2 then quantized variant if decode allocation exceeds threshold. patch: 
```
redwoodctl rollout create --route /v1/generate --fallback rdx-large-quantized --threshold 0.75
```
deploy-bot: rollout 56f4a initiated for /v1/generate by @Samir. Progressive canary starting (10% -> 50% -> 100%).
Javier Morales: started cordon/drain for nodes g-decode-07 and g-decode-09. Evicting affected pods and moving load to healthy nodes.
Priya Kaur: applying emergency throttle for burst concurrency: 
```
redwoodctl config set /routes/generate/concurrency_burst 4
```
Alice Nguyen: prepping handoff. I'm going to collect checklist: monitoring links, recent logs, mitigation commands, customer list.
Samir Patel: quick patch pushed to runtime: enforce in-request token cap before allocation (hotfix id hf-2025-11-02-01). It will reject requests with tokens >512 with a 400 and reason `tokencap_enforced`.
Maya Chen: support update: after cap applied error rate dropped to 18% then 6% over 8 minutes. Still seeing node restarts but fewer OOMs.
Javier Morales: observed node-level pod evictions stabilizing. OOMs per minute down from 18 to 2. GPU memory pressure reduced.
Ben Carter: AcmeCorp seeing improvement to ~10% failure; they've paused a heavy job while we stabilize.
Lina Ortiz: product asks for quick root cause notes: is this SDK default behavior or client-side bug?
Samir Patel: initial analysis: mix — some clients passed explicit null/0 or omitted max_tokens and our legacy default allowed unbounded. We'll update SDK defaults and server-side enforcement.
Alice Nguyen: handoff time: I'll hand to night oncall (Priya) at ts=3337779000. Priya, you'll take over monitoring cadence and customer comms.
Priya Kaur: confirmed. I'll be on for the next 12h. Please add runbook link and escalation contacts.
Alice Nguyen: runbook: https://internal.redwood/runbooks/decode-gpu-oom (added to thread). Also opened incident-1358 in tracker.
Samir Patel: post-incident actions assigned: SDK default change -> @Lina, server enforce tokencap -> @Samir, customer communications follow-up -> @Ben, postmortem owner -> @Javier.
deploy-bot: rollout 56f4a reached 100% at 3337779150.
Maya Chen: closing interim support update: most customers confirmed degraded but recovering. Continued monitoring for 2h.
Javier Morales: cordoned nodes uncordon scheduled after stabilization checks. If OOMs spike again, we'll rollback to reduced traffic mode.
Priya Kaur: taking handoff now. All actions and commands are in the runbook. I'll update metrics every 15m.
Alice Nguyen: thanks team. I'll file a brief incident summary for execs and add timeline.
incident-bot: INCIDENT: mitigations applied, paging suspended. Please continue to post updates in this thread for the next 2 hours. :white_check_mark:

title: #customer-success: 3265432100
preview:
incident-bot: ALERT: High error rate detected on /v1/generate for dedicated-cluster-5. Error rate spikes started ~2026-03-12T02:28Z. Pager on-call: @Samir. Automated mitigation: routing fallback applied to tier-2 models.
Samir Patel: Ack, joining bridge now. setting priority=high. will post brief status page copy for Maya to push.
Maya Lopez: thanks Samir — CS team needs: 1) short status-page blurb, 2) customer email draft (single para + short timeline), 3) confirmation on data exposure.
Samir Patel: quick update: rolled back last routing policy change (deploy id: route-2026-03-12-02). Fallback to model-variant b1 reduced errors from ~18% -> ~1.2% in 6m. Still seeing intermittent 429s for few tenants on cluster-5. Investigating root cause (suspect compile/kernel selection mismatch after that route change).
Ben Carter: saw surge in compile backlog on g4-pool around 02:26Z. log snippet: ```ERROR compile_queue_timeout model=redwood-opus-v2 shard=3 duration_ms=5020``` Backpressure propagated and caused generator to return 503/429.
Samir Patel: proposed status page blurb below — Maya feel free to shorten. Also adding a CS email draft after that.
Samir Patel: STATUS PAGE (proposed): 
- "Incident: Increased error rates for generation requests to dedicated-cluster-5. Mitigation: traffic routed to fallback model variants; errors substantially reduced. Impact: some requests may have experienced 5xx/429 responses between 02:25–02:40 UTC. Current status: degraded (mitigation in place)."
Samir Patel: CUSTOMER EMAIL DRAFT (proposed): 
"Hi Eli — we experienced increased errors affecting generation requests on your dedicated cluster for a ~15 minute window today (2026-03-12 02:25–02:40 UTC). We routed traffic to fallback model variants which reduced failed calls and restored normal service. We do not believe any customer data was exposed. We will share a full timeline, root cause analysis, and proposed preventative actions within 48 hours. Apologies for the disruption — Jordan will follow up with credit details and next steps.
— Samir & Redwood Support"
Jordan (CSM): draft looks good. For BrightApps can we state tentative credit: 10% of the invoice for the affected hour(s) or equivalent $ credit? Also add line: "If you'd like, we can schedule a 30-min call to walk the timeline and mitigation."
Maya Lopez: also add a one-line exec summary at top: "Service briefly degraded for a subset of dedicated customers; mitigation applied."
Eli (BrightApps): thanks — appreciate the quick mitigation. Two Qs: 1) timeline please, 2) was any user data or logs sent to 3rd-party?
Samir Patel: timeline (preliminary):
- 02:24: change route-2026-03-12-02 deployed to routing plane
- 02:25: compile backlog spike observed on g4-pool -> increased compile latency
- 02:26: first 5xx/429 observed for some tenants on cluster-5
- 02:31: automated routing fallback triggered, some traffic shifted to b1 variants
- 02:34: manual rollback of route-2026-03-12-02 completed (traffic restored to previous stable route)
- 02:40: error rate returned to baseline (~0.1%)
Samir Patel: on data exposure: we ran quick grep and audit of egress paths and 3rd-party integrations for affected instances — no evidence of data leaving Redwood boundaries. command used: `rg "upload_to_s3|external_callback" /var/logs/cluster-5 -n --no-ignore-vcs` returned no matches for the affected timeframe.
Ben Carter: remediation actions for post-incident roadmap: 1) add circuit-breaker on routing changes to prevent wide fanout of unvalidated rules, 2) increase compile workers for g4-pool during deploy windows, 3) add tighter compile-time SLO and early detection alerts.
Samir Patel: agreed. I'll capture these in the RCA doc. Action items:
- Samir: produce full RCA draft + prevention plan within 48h (assigned)
- Ben: run capacity check and propose autoscale tweak for g4-pool by EOD + add test for compile backlog alerts
- Jordan: prepare customer credit proposal for BrightApps (suggest 10% credit for impacted minute-window) and schedule follow-up call if Eli wants
- Maya: update status page with approved short blurb and link to follow-up RCA when published
Jordan (CSM): Eli — we propose a 10% credit for the impacted hour prorated across your usage that window. If that sounds OK we'll process it and include in the next billing cycle. Want to set a 30-min call Wednesday 10:00 UTC?
Eli (BrightApps): 30-min Wed 10:00 UTC works. Credit proposal sounds fair. Also please confirm whether the fallback model affected tokenization/outputs for ongoing prompts — any risk of silent semantic changes?
Samir Patel: fallback model b1 is a compatible variant with same tokenizer; we ran spot checks on customer prompts and found equivalent outputs within acceptable variance. No silent truncation or PII leaks discovered.
Jordan (CSM): noted — scheduling call and will send calendar invite. We'll send a short customer-facing timeline + credit confirmation after Samir posts the RCA draft.
Maya Lopez: thanks team. Closing notes: keep thread for RCA link + final status page URL. :thumbsup:
Samir Patel: will update thread with RCA link and final mitigation checklist when ready.
Jordan (CSM): thanks everyone.

title: #incidents: 3180501234
preview:
incident-bot: 🔔 Pager P1: Serving errors spike in hosted inference (error rate 15%, latency 95p > 1.5s). Auto-assigned to oncall: Aisha Khan.

Aisha Khan: acked, looking. seeing sustained 5xx on /v1/generate across us-west. Anyone on-call for autoscaler? @Miguel?

Miguel Ortiz: on it. I'm seeing lots of node flaps in the inference node pool over last 4 mins. kube-events show repeated OOMKills on inference pods then HPA scaling up then immediate restarts. Suspect rapid scale up/down feedback.

Priya Shah: logs from one pod: 
```
2026-03-15T10:03:15Z ERROR ServingRuntime - CUDA_ERROR_OUT_OF_MEMORY: failed to allocate 2.3G
2026-03-15T10:03:15Z INFO Reconnecting to model shard 0
2026-03-15T10:03:16Z WARN PodRestart - CrashLoopBackOff
```

Ben Carter: confirmed. HPA target CPU spikes to 90% then drops, HPA ups replicas to 12, but new pods hit OOM during initialization and cause node GPU memory fragmentation -> kubelet evicts older pods -> cascade.

Raj Patel: that's the feedback loop: autoscaler sees CPU/latency spike -> scales -> warmup alloc causes CUDA OOM -> pods crash -> metrics bounce -> more scaling. Need to break it.

oncall-eu: customers reporting 502s, some dedicated customers routed to dedicated capacity, hosted customers with bursty traffic affected. Current 5xx rate ~14.8% (15m average).

Aisha Khan: immediate mitigations: 1) pause HPA, 2) reduce target replicas to a safe steady-state, 3) drain/cordon problematic nodes, 4) route traffic to fallback pool. Miguel / Priya take the actions, I'll coordinate customer comms.

Miguel Ortiz: executing: `kubectl patch hpa inference-hpa -n prod -p '{"spec":{"minReplicas":3,"maxReplicas":3}}'` to freeze scaling to 3 (will force steady state). Also adding annotation to disable autoscaling controller sync temporarily.

Priya Shah: cordoning nodes with repeated GPU OOM: `kubectl cordon gke-infer-07 gke-infer-09` and `kubectl drain gke-infer-09 --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data`. Marking node as under-investigation.

SRE-bot: Deploy bot detected a config change 3180501248 by Miguel Ortiz: patched HPA min/max -> 3.

Ben Carter: switching routing: flipping canary traffic to dedicated pool for latency-sensitive routes (`/v1/stream` and `/v1/chat`). Patch applied in route table, currently shifting ~40% to dedicated. Monitoring error rate.

Aisha Khan: Product/Support: please prepare status update. Priya, what's the error-rate trend after HPA freeze & cordon?

Priya Shah: after freeze + cordon + route shift (5m in): overall 5xx dropped from 15% -> 6.2%. Latency 95p back to 1.0s. But a subset of long-running batches still failing on legacy model variants.

Miguel Ortiz: additional action: disable prefix batching for the affected model family to reduce peak GPU memory during startup. Patch to runtime: `REDWOOD_BATCHER_DISABLE_PREFIX=true` for those deployment sets. Rolling restart of 3 pods in place.

Raj Patel: for longer-term safety during the incident, I'm lowering the per-pod memory request for the quantized model to avoid warmup fragmentation and enabling on-boot gc sweep: `--gc-aggressive` flag for runtime. We'll track OOMs.

Ben Carter: Monitoring update: after these mitigations, error rate steady ~3-4% and dropping. No new node flaps in last 3 minutes. We should be able to re-enable autoscaling carefully with a dampening window.

Aisha Khan: agree. Plan to re-enable with conservative params: target min 4 max 8, scaleUp stabilization 300s, scaleDown stabilization 600s. We'll also enable model fallback to quantized variant when OOMs observed. Miguel to apply.

Miguel Ortiz: applying conservative HPA: `kubectl patch hpa inference-hpa -p '{"spec":{"minReplicas":4,"maxReplicas":8}}'` and setting controller annotation `scale-stabilization: 300s`. Patch in prod at 10:18Z.

SRE-bot: Alert: new pod OOMs dropped below threshold.

Priya Shah: customer impact notes: we have 12 customers with elevated errors (>5% of requests). Two enterprise tenants experienced degraded SLA for ~12 minutes. Support is drafting messages for those two.

Aisha Khan: draft OK to send? Keep it factual: describe transient capacity instability, mitigation steps, and an ETA for follow-up postmortem. Priya, Ben coordinate the messages and include temporary routing workaround for customers who need immediate relief.

Raj Patel: post-incident remediation items (initial): 1) add HPA dampening defaults to all inference HPAs, 2) add startup memory reservation to avoid GPU fragmentation, 3) implement runtime safe-start (pre-allocate smaller tensors during init), 4) create automated rollback policy to quantized variant on OOM spike.

Miguel Ortiz: logging snippet for SOC: 
```
k8s_events | grep inference | tail -n 20
2026-03-15T10:05:12Z Pod inference-aaa OOMKilled container runtime
2026-03-15T10:05:13Z HPA inference-hpa scale to 12
2026-03-15T10:05:15Z Pod inference-bbb CrashLoopBackOff
2026-03-15T10:06:00Z Cordon node gke-infer-09
2026-03-15T10:07:10Z Route update applied -> shift 40% to dedicated
```

Ben Carter: ops note: post-restore we'll run a controlled scale test (simulate slow ramp) in staging with the exact model weights to validate warmup behavior before enabling aggressive autoscaling again.

Aisha Khan: thanks team. I'm escalating to incident-review. We'll keep HPA conservative for 2h and schedule a postmortem. I'll send a status update to #customers-incident-updates in 10m with impact summary. Oncall handed over to Miguel at 10:25Z once stabilised.

Miguel Ortiz: handing off monitoring to Raj for the next 2 hours. If any OOMs spike, auto-route to dedicated pool immediately.

Raj Patel: ack. Adding a transient alert policy: if CUDA_OOM events > 5/min -> open pager.

incident-bot: Incident P1 status updated: mitigated, monitoring. Timeline: root 3180501234, mitigations applied 3180501290 - 3180501580. Postmortem required.

Aisha Khan: closing thread here. Add any follow-ups to the postmortem checklist ticket. Thanks all. :thumbsup:

title: #eng-oncall: 3071237890
preview:
Maya Patel: flagging a recurring problem: postmortem items are landing in backlog and not getting verified. Proposing a compact remediation schema + automated reminders + a stricter escalation ladder. Short discussion?

Diego Ruiz: yes please. the fewer free-text fields the better. also we should require a 'regression-check' that is automated (smoke test) so we don't rely on manual signoff.

Lena Ho: agree on automation. also we keep seeing the same failure modes (db-connection, cache-evict, auth-timeouts). can we tag those on the PM and feed a weekly digest to eng leads?

Maya Patel: sketch of a tiny YAML we can paste into a Jira comment — parseable and enforces minimal fields:

```
remediation: "short sentence"
owner: "Name (team)"
priority: "P0|P1|P2"
due: "YYYY-MM-DD"
failure_mode: [db-connection, cache-evict, infra-deg]
prevented_by: "what guardrail prevents recurrence"
regression_check: "smoke-suite-id or url"
checkpoints: "36h:status, 3d:status, 21d:status"
pm_link: "postmortem url"
notes: "optional"
```

Ben Carter: Nice. I like the 36h checkpoint (faster than 72h). Ops-bot can ping the owner and the channel if a checkpoint value is unchanged.

Diego Ruiz: escalation idea: if 36h missed -> notify owner + team channel; if 3d missed -> escalate to tech lead and auto-create follow-up card in remediation board; if 21d no regression_check -> open a bug and tag for backlog review.

Lena Ho: For IC rotation: propose 1-week IC shifts with a daytime shadow for the last day that does handoff + a one-pager checklist. IC should pick comms cadence on declare: minimal template: initial, 30m, major-update, final. Keep updates under 3 lines and link to timeline.

Maya Patel: Also: require a recurrence assessment field: "short-term fix? yes/no" and "long-term action? yes/no" so we can prioritize systemic work. If long-term=yes, assign a follow-up owner and schedule a triage with platform in 7d window.

Ben Carter: operational notes: ops-bot can support reminders, tag extracts, and weekly heatmap. I can wire a small lambda to scan Jira comments for the YAML and emit metrics.

ops-bot: Reminder created: checkpoint 36h for REM-2024-455 (owner: Maya Patel) will post in #eng-oncall at 3071239290.

Diego Ruiz: governance q: who enforces SLA? suggestion: team lead owns enforcement; IC owns the immediate remediation pacing during incident -> to avoid split responsibilities.

Lena Ho: also add a tiny field: "verification: automated/manual" so dashboard can surface what still needs human follow-up.

Maya Patel: Action items from thread:
- Ben: prototype YAML extractor + weekly failure-mode digest (owner: Ben, due: 10 days)
- Lena: write the IC 1-week shift handoff + one-pager checklist (owner: Lena, due: 4 days)
- Diego: draft escalation ladder and a short runbook paragraph for SLA enforcement (owner: Diego, due: 3 days)
- Maya: update REM-2024-455 with the YAML and trigger the 36h checkpoint (owner: Maya, due: today)

Ben Carter: confirmed. I'll make a repo and share the link when ready.

Lena Ho: confirmed, will push to the oncall docs.

Diego Ruiz: confirmed.

Maya Patel: thanks all. if you want to pair on the extractor, I'll be online tomorrow 11am PT.

ops-bot: Escalation rule recorded: 36h -> owner ping, 72h -> tech lead escalation, 21d -> backlog bug creation. Recorded to policy-store v1.2.

title: #eng-releases: 2623456789
preview:
Alex Kim: kicking off v2 gateway phased rollout during the 03:00–06:00 change window. plan: 5% -> 20% -> 60% -> 100% with 20m observation windows. watch: mobile SDKs, EU region, and client pin header behavior.
Maria Gonzales: release notes drafted and uploaded. callout: clients relying on implicit header behavior need to add explicit X-API-VERSION. link: https://go/release-notes/v2
Ben Carter: noted. I've highlighted the behavioural change in generation truncation and the compatibility section. also added rollback triggers.
Samira Patel: quick check — are we forcing 1.0 clients to remain on v1 via routing? we saw ~2% calls without any version header.
Alex Kim: yes. routing rule: if header absent AND client-agent matches legacy list -> route to v1. ops will seed the client-pin rules now.
deploy-bot: Deploy started: service=api-gateway target=canary-wave-1 traffic=5% at 2623456792.
SRE Ops: monitors in place. alert thresholds: 5xx rate >0.5% sustained 10m, p95 latency >300ms, or error budget burn >10% in 1h.
Maria Gonzales: add quality gate: token-level regression >3% on our prompt set triggers an automatic pause.
Ben Carter: adding that to the deploy manifest and release notes.
mobile-team: FYI mobile SDK team will cut release 1.5 in ~2h with header support. we'll backport to 1.4 patch if needed.
Samira Patel: clients we care about: mobile-android <=1.3, mobile-ios <=1.2, embedded SDKs older than 2024-09. list shared in drive.
Alex Kim: if deploy-bot reports >10 distinct client pins within a wave, auto-pause and notify eng-releases. manual override only after triage.
deploy-bot: client-pin-detected: 7 distinct client versions reporting X-Client-Pin=1. Pausing traffic shift at wave=1 at 2623456815. Details: mobile-android:1.3 (4 clients), mobile-ios:1.2 (3 clients).
Alex Kim: pausing rollout. Ops, please roll canary down to 0% and revert any routing changes that sent legacy clients to v2.
SRE Ops: executing rollback sequence now: set canary traffic 5% -> 0%, apply legacy pin routing. ETA 2m.
deploy-bot: Canary rollback completed at 2623456830. Reason: client pins detected (see above).
Ben Carter: updating release notes with an explicit "paused" banner and instructions for SDK owners + customers.
Maria Gonzales: scheduling 30m sync to decide next steps: wait for SDK 1.5 rollout or widen compatibility shim. I'll ping calendars.
Alex Kim: thanks. outcome options to prepare for the sync: (1) resume staggered rollout after 1.5 release and 24h telemetry, (2) implement temporary routing shim to respect X-Client-Pin indefinitely, (3) full rollback and postpone release. document pros/cons.
Samira Patel: logging action items: ops applied routing pin, mobile-team cutting patch, docs updated, eng-releases on-call notified.
mobile-team: on it — will post PR and timeline in the sync.
Alex Kim: great work. keep the thread updated with deploy-bot outputs and any client escalation requests.

title: #incidents: 2419001234
preview:
incident-bot: PagerDuty alert fired: PD#INC-4821 — auth-token-validate error rate > 5% in prod. Trigger: auth-validate service -> 'kid not found' (JWT). Created channel #incident-4821. Alert threshold breached at ~2026-03-14T11:03:00Z. Link: https://pagerduty.example/INC-4821 :rotating_light:

pagerduty: Alert details: service=auth-validate, env=prod, metric=token_validation_error_rate, value=7.8%, window=5m. Automatic page sent to oncall.

Alice Nguyen: Acked — I'm oncall and pulling this in. Triage starting.

Irene Sato: I see a flood of "kid not found" in auth-validate at the LB layer. Sample log:
```
[2026-03-14T11:02:58Z] auth-validate: warn request_id=abc123 uid=svc-xyz error="jwt: no matching key (kid 3e4f)" path=/v1/chat
[2026-03-14T11:02:59Z] auth-validate: warn request_id=def456 uid=svc-xyz error="jwt: no matching key (kid 3e4f)" path=/v1/generate
```
Possible cause: jwks cache not updated after key rotation. Checking jwks-cache + key-service.

Ben Carter: I'm awake, joining. Any key rotation events around 10:58?

Alice Nguyen: Yes, there was an automated key rotation at 2026-03-14T10:58:10Z (key-service/rotate job). It reports success but I see spikes of 502/504 from jwks-cache to key-service in the control plane logs.

Irene Sato: Running quick checks: 
`kubectl logs deployment/jwks-cache --since=10m | grep -E "error|timeout|504" | tail -n 20` shows repeated timeouts to key-service. Also `curl -s https://jwks-cache.prod.internal/.well-known/jwks.json | jq -c '.'` returns an empty object intermittently.

Irene Sato: Hypothesis: key-service rotated and returned new JWKS with new kid (3e4f) but jwks-cache instances didn't fetch it due to transient errors -> auth-validate attempting verification against cache sees missing kid -> fails.

Ben Carter: Mitigation options: (1) Force jwks-cache to bypass cache & fetch directly from key-service, (2) enable fallback acceptance of previous key in auth-validate for short window, (3) rollback key rotation (promote previous key) if propagation cannot be restored quickly.

Alice Nguyen: I'm going to flush jwks-cache instances first (lowest impact). Executing: `kubectl exec -n infra jwks-cache-0 -- curl -XPOST http://localhost:8080/flush` and same for -1/-2.

Irene Sato: Flushed jwks-cache across all pods, 11:12 UTC. Patch applied to auth-validate to accept multiple kids temporarily: `kubectl set env deployment/auth-validate ALLOW_FALLBACK_KIDS=true` (non-intrusive, no restart).

CS-ops: Noted. We're tracking affected customers: several mid-market tenants showing spikes in 401 on generate/chat. CS will prepare a proactive note once we have expected ETA.

pagerduty: Incident acknowledged by Alice Nguyen at 2026-03-14T11:13:04Z.

Irene Sato: After flush and env change, error rate dropped sharply. Metrics: token_validation_error_rate -> 0.9% in next 5m (was 7.8%). Logs now show both old and new kids present in jwks-cache.

Ben Carter: Good. Root cause likely a transient failure path in jwks-cache where retries/backoff caused a brief window with empty keys. Follow-ups: add stricter monitoring on jwks-cache fetch latency, add golden-record sanity check after rotations, and add immediate cache invalidation hooks post-rotation.

Alice Nguyen: Doing handoff to Ben Carter for 12:00-14:00 UTC coverage while I compile timeline and customer impact. Please document any changes; we've already set ALLOW_FALLBACK_KIDS=true — we'll remove that once healthy for 1 hour.

incident-bot: On-call handover: Alice Nguyen -> Ben Carter at 2026-03-14T12:00:00Z.

Ben Carter: Monitoring. Observed sustained error rate <1% for 20m. Will keep open until 13:00 UTC and then close if stable. Mitigation timeline:
- 11:03 detect
- 11:10 triage
- 11:12 cache flush + fallback enabled
- 11:20 error rate back to normal

CS-ops: Drafted outbound note to customers, marking incident as "service interruption (auth token validation) affecting API traffic". ETA for full postmortem: EOD tomorrow.

Irene Sato: Post-incident tasks (adding to Jira):
- AUTH-928: add immediate cache invalidation webhook to key-service (priority P0)
- AUTH-929: strengthen jwks-cache retry/backoff to prefer short-circuit on rotation events (P1)
- AUTH-930: add canary checks after rotation that verify keys exist in all jwks-cache replicas (P1)

Ben Carter: I'll schedule a follow-up incident review at 2026-03-15T09:30Z to go over telemetry and rollout changes.

Alice Nguyen: Closing notes: temporary mitigation ALLOW_FALLBACK_KIDS=true will be reverted at 2026-03-14T13:15Z unless we see regressions. Final root cause suspected: transient network/503s from control-plane -> jwks-cache missed a rotation window.

incident-bot: Incident status update: resolved (mitigated) at 2026-03-14T11:23:17Z by Ben Carter.

Ben Carter: Confirmed stable for 60m. Marking incident resolved and will own postmortem. Good job team :thumbsup:

Alice Nguyen: Thanks all. Will attach logs, graphs, and exact commands to the Jira and postmortem doc.

CS-ops: Communicated to customers at 11:45 UTC. No escalations so far.

Irene Sato: Leaving final monitoring watch for another hour then archive the channel.

Ben Carter: archiving at 12:24 UTC if no more alerts.

incident-bot: Channel archived."

title: #incidents: 2381234567
preview:
IncidentsBot: :rotating_light: Alert: authz-service > error-rate threshold (token-verification failures). Suspected upstream Okta JWKS origin latency. Paging @Priya Shah (on-call) — please acknowledge.
Priya Shah: acked, looking. Which services are showing errors?
Alice Nguyen: seeing a spike in verifier errors across auth-proxy and console routes. Example logs from verifier:
Alice Nguyen: ```ERROR verifier: signature verification failed: key not found (kid: abc123) - upstream jwks fetch status=504 time=1200ms``` 
Alice Nguyen: Console 403s start ~2381234500, token-introspect calls to Okta are timing out or returning 5xx.
Samir Patel: I'm hitting Okta JWKS endpoint directly: `curl -i https://company.okta.com/oauth2/v1/keys` -> intermittent 504s and long TTFB (~1.3s). Possibly origin rate-limiting during morning sync.
Ben Carter: Confirmed. Our jwks cache miss + synchronous fetch path is blowing up under thundering herd from many short-lived tokens. Cache TTLs are low (30s). We need immediate mitigation to avoid console-wide login failures.
Priya Shah: Mitigation options?
Ben Carter: Fast mitigations in order:
- bump local jwks cache TTL to 5m
- enable verifier local-key-fallback (use last-good JWKS from disk instead of failing)
- change console auth route to prefer session cookies and skip remote introspect for browser flows (falls back to our existing session validator)
Ben Carter: I can push a quick roll (config change) for cache TTL and toggle fallback. Command:
Ben Carter: ```
# set feature flag
curl -X POST https://config.internal/api/flags -H 'Authorization: Bearer $OP' -d '{"flag":"auth.okta_local_fallback","value":true}'
# update cache TTL
curl -X POST https://config.internal/api/services/auth-proxy -d '{"jwks_cache_ttl":300}'
``` 
Priya Shah: Do it. Also add a temporary route weight to prefer dedicated auth-delta instances (less load) for console.
Ben Carter: applying config now. Pushing flag -> :white_check_mark:
Ben Carter: TTL updated -> :white_check_mark:
Marcus Lee: After the TTL bump, I see jwks fetch rate drop and error-rate decreasing. Verifier still failing for some tokens with kid mismatch; last-good-jwks fallback served a lot of verifications.
Alice Nguyen: Observability note: increased 5xx from Okta between 2381234480-2381234560, peak at 2381234522. Console 403s aligned. Starting to see gradual recovery in the last 30s.
Elena Morales: From support: ~12 unique customers reported SSO/console login issues, highest impact for customers in EU region. Have we drafted a short status page update?
Priya Shah: Yes. Suggested status message: "We are experiencing degraded login service for some customers due to intermittent issues with our auth provider. We have applied mitigations and are monitoring. Some users may need to re-login. ETA to full recovery TBD." Want me to publish?
Elena Morales: Please post. I'll coordinate outbound comms for active customers.
Samir Patel: For longer-term: implement jittered refresh + asynchronous background JWKS refresh to avoid synchronous origin fetch on cache miss. Also consider HSM-signed fallback keys for emergency verification.
Ben Carter: Agreed. I'll open follow-ups: #1 async jwks refresh, #2 persist last-good-jwks to disk on every successful fetch, #3 increase default TTL and add jitter. 
Priya Shah: Handoff: I'm staying on incident until stable. Marcus, can you take on next 45m monitoring shift? I'll update pager and escalate to SRE lead if errors creep back up.
Marcus Lee: taking it. I'll watch error-rate and alert if >3% for 5m.
Priya Shah: Thanks — publishing status now. :thumbsup:
IncidentsBot: Status update posted to status.redwood.ai (incident-2381234567). Ticket created: INC-2026-238
Elena Morales: I'll send an email to affected customers with the status link and a short description.
Alice Nguyen: Postmortem items captured: root cause likely Okta origin saturation plus low TTL + synchronous fetch path. Remediation planned as Ben listed. Will draft RCA after incident window closes.
Priya Shah: last check -> error rates steady and console logins recovering. Marking incident as monitoring. Marcus, escalate if regression.
Marcus Lee: acknowledged.

title: #devex: 2301234567
preview:
Tara Lin: quick UX note — the new mini-preview row in the playground is promising but opening the schema still pushes the run history. during quick debugging that shift is distracting. can we avoid reflow?

Owen Gupta: Hmm, I ran into the same. Proposal: keep a fixed-height row for each fn with a 1-line summary (name + type badges + small chevron). expand should either layer an overlay anchored to that row or expand inline without changing page height.

Marta Ruiz: Overlay would preserve scroll position but may block copy/paste. Inline-but-no-reflow could work if the expansion overlays only the schema area (absolute positioned) and doesn't affect the DOM flow. that requires z-index management.

Ben Carter: agree with Marta. Also: button copy is inconsistent across the app — we have "Run", "Invoke", "Start". i vote for "Run" everywhere. small copy PR will be quick.

Tara Lin: a11y: screen reader announces "expanded" but not which function. need aria-label like "Schema preview for function SummarizeText (expanded)" and an aria-live polite update. and when entering edit mode focus should jump into the first input. preview expand shouldn't grab focus.

A11yCheckBot: Created issue #620: "playground mini-preview - aria-labels + focus rules". Checklist: [x] verbose aria-label, [ ] focus on primary input when editing, [ ] aria-live polite region for state change.

Owen Gupta: param chips in run history would help scanning: tiny icons + short label (TXT/NUM/FILE) at 14px, high contrast. they should be semantic with aria-labels like "title: text".

Marta Ruiz: visual guideline: chips use filled icon + 12px label, 16px line-height. hover shows full value. keyboard: chips should be reachable via tab when a run entry is focused.

Ben Carter: dev note: please standardize the run keybinding. add `R` shortcut in playground and show tooltip "Press R to run". i can add the tooltip copy if everyone's ok with `R`.

Tara Lin: poll: collapse previews by default (power users) or expanded by default (new users)? reply with :one: collapse, :two: expanded.

Owen Gupta: :one:

Marta Ruiz: :one: but detect first run -> expanded

Ben Carter: :one:

A11yCheckBot: Poll result recorded: 3 votes collapse, 0 expanded. Recommendation: default collapsed; auto-expand for first-time.

Owen Gupta: runtime caveat: collapsing result previews must not evict KV cache needed for replays. i'll open a runtime ticket to confirm cache TTL semantics.

Marta Ruiz: small copy tweak suggestion: change label from "Schema (auto-detect)" to "Schema preview — inferred" and add microcopy when detection fails: "Couldn't infer schema. Edit to continue."

Tara Lin: thanks — i'll draft the interaction spec: mini-row layout, overlay vs inline overlay rules, focus behavior, param chip ARIA patterns, and localStorage flag for preference. will link prototype.

Ben Carter: i'll take the copy PR once Tara posts the spec.

A11yCheckBot: Linked tickets and TODOs to project board: #620 (a11y), #621 (param chips), #622 (kv-runtime).

title: #incidents: 2187654321
preview:
Alice Nguyen: Heads up — I've been tracking the DB connection incidents (3 in ~6 weeks). Before we lock the postmortem fixes, we should align on customer-facing comms and whether we change RCA-sharing policy. Quick sync?

Ben Carter: +1. We should also decide if recurring patterns get different comms cadence. Any consolidated pattern notes yet?

Priya Shah: Pulled a short summary: https://docs.redwood/pm/INC-DBCONN-summary — pattern: brief spikes during batch windows, similar failure signature each time. I flagged common traces in the doc. :eyes:

Marco Ruiz: The pattern looks actionable. For statuspage language I recommend we move from generic "service degraded" to something like "intermittent connectivity affecting a subset of requests" — more specific without being alarmist. Thoughts?

Elena Park: Agree on specificity. Also we need a clear rule for what we share in public RCAs vs private customer RCAs (redacted vs full). Last time we over-redacted and customers asked for more detail.

Alice Nguyen: Proposed checklist to decide now:
1) Statuspage phrasing update (ops to draft new sentence)
2) 72-hour customer summary template (short, non-technical) with optional detailed 7-day RCA for affected customers
3) Policy for sharing full RCAs: provide on request with consent + legal review for non-Enterprise accounts
Any objections?

Priya Shah: No objections. Action plan suggestion:
- Priya: draft statuspage sentence + 72h template by EOD Tue
- Marco: run deeper pattern analysis, attach mitigation recommendations by Fri
- Ben: update comms playbook with consent flow & tags by next Mon
- Elena: produce redaction checklist and security sign-off timeline (target Thu)
I can create the tracker tasks now.

incident-bot: Created follow-up tasks: #INC-DB-1001 (statuspage draft) assigned to Priya Shah, due 2187710000; #INC-DB-1002 (pattern analysis) assigned to Marco Ruiz, due 2187752000; #INC-DB-1003 (comms-playbook update) assigned to Ben Carter, due 2187810000.

Ben Carter: I'll also add a "recurring-pattern" tag to related postmortems and add an automated weekly dashboard. Proposal: if we see >=2 similar incidents within 30 days, trigger auto-escalation to Eng-Platform and Product for a prioritized mitigation ticket.

Marco Ruiz: Sounds good. I'll include detection thresholds and sample queries in the pattern analysis. Also proposing one-line summary snippets for the statuspage and for the 72h note. Example draft (for ops to tweak):

```
Proposed statuspage blurb: "We experienced intermittent connection failures in our inference layer affecting a subset of requests. We've deployed mitigations and are monitoring."
```

Elena Park: Security is okay with the high-level blurb. For any full RCA requested by a customer we require: (a) redaction checklist applied, (b) legal approval if customer requests unredacted logs, (c) support to record explicit consent. I'll post the checklist by Thu.

Alice Nguyen: Good. For public-facing timeline: short email/update in 72h and an internal detailed 7-day RCA. If affected customers request more, route to Support + Legal and then share under consent.

Priya Shah: I'll draft the 72h template and put it in #product-comms. Quick template idea: 1) What happened (plain language), 2) Impact (who/what), 3) Mitigation taken, 4) Next steps + ETA. Keep it <= 3 bullets.

Ben Carter: Owners and ETAs recap: Priya (statuspage + 72h) by Tue EOD, Marco (pattern analysis) by Fri, Ben (playbook + tags) by next Mon, Elena (security redaction) by Thu, Alice (customer outreach coordination) monitors. Internal playbook update by next Mon; public statuspage text aimed for Wed.

Marco Ruiz: Do we want a short follow-up sync to confirm wording and playbook edits? 30m tomorrow works for me.

Priya Shah: I'm free 10–11am PT.

Ben Carter: 11–12 PT works for me.

Alice Nguyen: Let's do 11:00am PT — I'll send a calendar invite and add the agenda: 1) statuspage text, 2) 72h template, 3) consent flow steps, 4) pattern detection thresholds.

incident-bot: Calendar invite created: "Pattern-informed comms playbook review" — 11:00 PT tomorrow.

Alice Nguyen: Thanks all — please keep updates in the tracker and tag me if anything slips. I'll consolidate final wording after the sync. :thumbsup:

title: #all-hands: 2123456789
preview:
Priya Rao: Hey team — heads up: doing a last-minute slides handoff at 10:05 PT and the cloud recorder in us-west is dropping segments (seen 2x 30s gaps). Can someone take a quick look and confirm fallback plan?
Devon Kim: On it. Seeing SRT ingest retry errors on the dashboard. Might be the CDN edge flapping.
ops-bot: Alert: recorder-service-us-west — 502 gateway errors x3 in last 5m. Incident ID: REC-WEST-2123.
Emma Torres: If we can't stabilize, propose we start local OBS capture on the stage laptop and have someone upload after. Who can run presigned upload?
Sam Wu: I can start OBS and record. Which settings?
Priya Rao: Sam — capture desktop, 1080p30, keyframe 2s, high bitrate (8–12mbps). We'll trim later. Also we still need captions live.
Ben Carter: Captioning is via live-caption service in the cloud region that's affected. If recorder is flapping, the captioning websocket might drop too. We have an alternate caption instance in eu-central (higher latency). Could bridge captions via relay.
Carla Nguyen: Quick workaround: start local OBS record + enable local SRT output to our staging ingest (srt://10.9.8.7:9000) — that'll bypass the CDN. Need someone to flip the route in the mixer.
Devon Kim: I can flip the mixer route. Command: 
```
# edge route switch (ops)
/api/v1/routing/switch --from=cdn-west --to=staging-ingest --id=rec-west-2123
```
Devon Kim: Doing the switch now — monitoring logs.
ops-bot: Action: routing change initiated by Devon Kim at 2123456810.
Priya Rao: Emma, Ben — if captions are relayed from eu-central we might see a 4–6s offset. Call it out to hosts so they pause briefly after slides change.
Emma Torres: Will tell hosts to hold 3–5s after slide flips. Also I'll pin the slide deck link in #all-hands and enable manual slide advance on my end if the automated handoff fails.
Sam Wu: OBS started and recording locally. Using settings Priya suggested. Uploaded test clip to staging. Link: https://files.internal/redwood/tmp/aw2h5.mp4
Carla Nguyen: Mixer route confirm: switched to staging-ingest 10.9.8.7:9000. Monitoring RTP packets — stable for 30s.
Devon Kim: Recorder logs now showing continuous write, no 502s for last 90s.
Priya Rao: Amazing. Can someone monitor the cloud recorder and caption websocket for the next 10 minutes during the handoff? Tag me if anything spikes.
Ben Carter: Monitoring captions — will switch relay to eu-central if websocket drops more than twice.
Emma Torres: Hosts briefed. Slide deck pinned. Starting Q&A collection doc and shared link here: https://docs.internal/redwood/allhands/qa-live
Priya Rao: Thank you all. If anything crops up, @here ping me. We'll debrief after the call on what caused the recorder flaps and put a permanent fallback in the runbook. :thumbsup:
