title: #incidents: 2051234567
preview:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: PagerDuty alert fired: service=apse1-az2-serving severity=high reason=cpu AND queue_latency spike. PD incident link: https://pagerduty.example/incidents/PD-83421 - automated page to oncall

pagerduty: Incident PD-83421 auto-assigned to Maya Chen (primary oncall). Escalation schedule in effect.

Maya Chen: acked PD-83421 :white_check_mark: I'm looking. Observability shows 95th CPU at 98% on ae-worker-03 and request queue growing.

Luis Ortega: seeing a lot of GRPC retry loops from routing-proxy in az2. kubeeviction rate high for the inference pods?

Maya Chen: checking node metrics now. `kubectl get pods -n redwood-serving -o wide --selector=zone=az2` shows a few pods in CrashLoopBackoff and multiple pods at OOMKilled.

Priya Nair: any live customer impact? Claire (Support) do we have tickets or chats flagging errors?

Claire (Support): a handful of 500s from eu-customer-42 and one enterprise tenant hitting timeouts on generation calls. Support flagged priority-to-sales for the enterprise one.

Ben Carter: logs show repeated `grpc: error: code = ResourceExhausted desc = KV cache eviction` from runtime. Looks like K/V cache GC thrashing after memory pressure.

Maya Chen: hypothesis: sudden shard imbalance + node flapping leading to high cold-cache ops -> memory spike -> evictions -> more cold cache. Initiating mitigation: scale up reserved pool in az2 and activate cross-region fallback.

Maya Chen: running command to bump pool: ```
# scale dedicated pool in az2
infractl pool scale --name apse1-az2-serving --replicas 6
```

deploy-bot: Received scale request by Maya Chen. Scaling apse1-az2-serving to 6 replicas. Progress: 2/6 ready.

Luis Ortega: while autoscaler spins up new replicas we should enable routing fallback to apse1-az1 and eu-west-1 for new requests. Adding routing rule now.

Maya Chen: enabling fallback policy (weight-based) - only for non-sticky sessions and new requests. Command: ```
redwoodctl route update --service apse1-serve --fallback "apse1-az1:0.5,eu-west-1:0.5" --conditions "region==apse1 AND zone==az2 AND latency>200ms"
```

deploy-bot: Routing policy updated. New requests from az2 will be routed to fallback pool per rule.

Priya Nair: notify Observability and customer success. I'll draft status for the incident channel and customers. Claire please collect affected request IDs and tenant IDs.

Claire (Support): collecting now. Two customers with elevated error rates (tenant ids: T-3385, T-7761). Will open interim status emails.

Maya Chen: PD-83421: ack still active. I'm triggering canary failover for low-priority traffic to eu-west-1 to verify model compatibility. Model variant: `redwood-open-gpt-medium-quant` (compat-fallback).

Ben Carter: note: quantized variant has ~5-10% perf delta on some prompts. We'll monitor quality metrics.

Luis Ortega: adding temporary token bucket to limit bursty batch producers in az2 to reduce pressure: ```
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: az2-burst-control
data:
  max_inflight: "150"
EOF
```

Maya Chen: good. Observability: queue length starting to drop after routing update. CPU on ae-worker-03 down from 98% -> 72% in last 4m. New pods coming up.

pagerduty: Reminder: if incident not acknowledged in 15m escalate to Luis Ortega (secondary).

Maya Chen: I'm handing off active management to Luis for stabilization and postmortem. Luis, I'm leaving notes in the incident runbook (see /incidents/runbooks/apse1-az2-memory).

Luis Ortega: taking over. I'll keep PD updated and coordinate rollback if quality regression observed.

Priya Nair: quick customer note: "We've identified a regional serving degradation in ap-southeast-1 (az2). We've routed new requests to fallback pools; affected customers may see slightly different latency or model version. We're monitoring quality and will provide updates every 15 minutes." Approve?

Maya Chen: approved, send. Add mention of specific tenant impact T-3385/T-7761.

Claire (Support): status message sent to affected customers and flagged escalation to account team for T-7761.

Ben Carter: monitoring for regressions: running a quick eval set against fallback model. Initial diffs show 97% parity — acceptable for emergency routing.

Luis Ortega: OK. plan: 1) let scaled nodes stabilize 2) monitor KV cache hit ratio and GC 3) if stable after 30m, restore normal routing and drain fallback 4) postmortem to cover shard balancing + autoscaler tuning.

Maya Chen: adding timeline to runbook and creating Jira EPIC: INF-8239 (postmortem + mitigations).

deploy-bot: Scale complete: 6/6 replicas ready for apse1-az2-serving.

Maya Chen: great — begin draining fallback after we see 10m stable metrics.

Luis Ortega: marking PD-83421 as under-control, will set to monitored.

pagerduty: PD-83421 status changed to "under_control" by Luis Ortega.

Maya Chen: follow-up actions for postmortem: - investigate autoscaler thresholds and OOM patterns - add guardrails for KV cache burst - improve shard rebalancer to avoid hotspots - consider proactive warm-up when autoscaling events predicted

Priya Nair: I'll coordinate customer follow-ups and update the incident summary. Next update in 15 minutes unless anything changes. :hourglass_flowing_sand:

title: #docs: 1989002345
preview:

Ben Carter: yeah, I hit that in a support ticket last week. users copy examples from the quickstart and get 400s because the SDK expects snake_case in python.

title: #eng-ml: 1965000000
preview:
Maya Patel: Quick sanity check — bringing up qint8 reranker (rerank-model-qint8-v2) on the prod holdout and seeing overall NDCG@10 drop ~1.8% vs fp16. Split by query length shows a bigger hit on long queries. Anyone else see a length-dependent regression?

Owen Li: Noted. What's the holdout? global shuffle or time-based?

Maya Patel: time-based holdout last 7 days. Holdout id `holdout_2026-03-14`. Eval command:

```bash
python eval_rerank.py --model rerank-qint8-v2 --holdout holdout_2026-03-14.json --split-by query_length --buckets "0-32,33-128,129-512" --metrics ndcg@10,mrr
```

Maya Patel: Quick paste of bucketed diff (fp16 -> qint8):

```
0-32   ndcg@10 0.726 -> 0.718 (-1.1%)
33-128 ndcg@10 0.642 -> 0.613 (-4.5%)
129-512 ndcg@10 0.588 -> 0.507 (-13.8%)
```

Ben Carter: Whoa large drop for 129-512. Could be tokenization/truncation differences or KV-cache alignment issue during quantized decoding.

Owen Li: Did you re-run with fixed doc-order and no randomized padding? That can bias long-query samples.

Maya Patel: Re-ran with deterministic padding/left-truncation and same tokenizer version — same trend but slightly less severe (129-512: -11%).

ci-bot: perf-eval/1965000000 started by @maya; results uploaded to https://redwood.artifacts/rerank/evals/1965000000

Ben Carter: Can you dump per-example logits for a subset? If quantization shifts logits scale, NDCG changes can be amplified for longer queries where scores are tighter.

Maya Patel: Yeah uploading 1k examples. Also noticed calibration delta: qint8 scores have ~0.6 sigma lower spread. Thinking to try post-quant calib (mean/var rescale) per-batch.

Owen Li: Try temperature scaling or isotonic mapping trained on small dev split. Also compare MRR — if MRR flat but NDCG drops, means top-rank reshuffle for long tails.

Maya Patel: MRR change: overall -2.0%, 129-512 bucket MRR -9.4% so not just tail.

Ben Carter: Another hypothesis: attention masking bug on longer sequences after kernel swap. Put together a micro-batch reproducer with seq_len=320 and compare kv shapes between fp16 and qint8.

Maya Patel: Will run reproducer. Also plan to try qint8 with per-channel scale vs global scale to see impact.

Owen Li: Good. Keep notes for the rollout hold. If we can't close <5% in long queries, block canary for models serving >50% long-query traffic.

Maya Patel: Agreed. I'll post updates. Thanks.

title: #announcements: 1956000000
preview:
Omar Aziz: FYI team — publishing the Private GDPR & Data Handling playbook today. This is for customers using Private/VPC installs who asked for clear guidance on subject requests, export controls, and telemetry settings.
Priya Rao: Runbook link: https://intra.redwood/runbooks/private-gdpr-playbook (draft). Contains step-by-step: responding to DSARs, configuring residency, and audit export samples.
Ben Carter: Key tech notes: we added two new controller endpoints: POST /dpa/acknowledge (for customer DPA acceptance) and GET /audit/export-status. Installer flag to disable telemetry by default is --telemetry=off. Example: 
```
./installer --mode private --telemetry=off --residency eu-central-1
```
Sofia N.: Legal + CSM: playbook includes email templates for DSAR acknowledgements and a customer checklist to prove data residency during audits. Please review the templates and suggest edits.
Omar Aziz: Ops: upgrades are online. For customers on agent <=v1.9 there is a small migration command: 
```
sudo redwood-agent backup-config && sudo redwood-agent upgrade --preserve-kv
```
Expect ~20-30m per node on average.
Priya Rao: Compliance note: default telemetry retention for new private installs is 30 days; customers can change to 0/30/90/365 via the Audit API. We added example payloads in the runbook.
Ben Carter: Security: Audit exports now include a signed provenance header. For on-prem HSMs, rotation automation remains opt-in and will emit an audit event with key metadata.
deploy-bot: Release schedule updated: playbook published -> 1956003600, customer emails to go out after legal sign-off at 1956089200. See #release-war-room for rollout.
Omar Aziz: Action items: 1) Legal & CSM (Sofia + Legal) finalise DSAR email text by EOD Wednesday. 2) Eng (Ben) verify installer flag in air-gapped bundle. 3) Ops (Priya) confirm per-node upgrade times from two pilot customers. Ping this thread when done.

title: #eng-runtime: 1952346789
preview:
Alice Nguyen: Heads-up — after yesterday's continuous-batcher tweak (mini-window=16 -> 8) we're seeing a 3x p95 spike on short requests (~<=64 tokens). Noticed around 10:45 UTC.
Ben Carter: Confirming, saw it in perf dashboards. Throughput also dipped for small-seq traffic. p50 is fine. p95 and p99 are terrible.
perf-bot: Alert: continuous-batcher-regression detected in prod cluster eu-west-1. Metric: latency_p95_up 320% over baseline. Triggered at 2026-03-18T10:46:12Z.
Carla Mendez: Pushed a targeted profiler run on a replica. Attaching trimmed traces — most samples in "prepare_batch" waiting on queue policy, then kernel selection falls back to slow path.
Dylan Ho: Any clue why kernel selection flips? mini-window smaller -> more batches -> maybe different batch shapes? Could be kernel affinity thresholds.
Alice Nguyen: Here's a minimal repro I ran locally against the canary: ```bash
abatch-client --target=canary:8000 --seq-len=32 --qps=120 --duration=5m
```Results: p95 jump after ~20s.
Frank Li: I pulled a flamegraph snapshot. Key hot path: continuous_batch::form_batches -> check_kernel_compat -> fallback_to_generic_kernel. Lots of contention on kernel_locks.
Eva Singh: Also seeing KV cache churn metrics increase. Cache hit-rate down ~12% for those short requests. Could be because smaller windows break prefix coalescing.
Ben Carter: That matches: smaller mini-window reduces prefix sharing opportunities -> more unique KV keys -> eviction thrash on hosts with limited KV slots.
Carla Mendez: Dump of the profiler snippet: ```
# top functions
30% prepare_batch
22% check_kernel_compat
18% generic_kernel_exec
10% kv_cache_lookup
auth_stats: kernel_lock_waits=0.9ms/op
```We should fix kernel selection early to avoid generic path.
Dylan Ho: Quick fix options: 1) revert mini-window to 16 (rollout revert), 2) tweak kernel affinity to prefer small-seq optimized kernels even if batch shapes vary, 3) raise KV cache capacity on canary.
Alice Nguyen: Can we A/B 2) first? Lower blast radius. If affinity change is safe, we can roll to canary and watch perf-bot.
Frank Li: Affinity tweak: reduce compatibility threshold from 0.85 -> 0.7 so specialized kernels accept slightly more shapes. I'll prepare a small patch.
deploy-bot: Deploy job queued: ref=affinity-tweak-0.7 -> canary (3 nodes). Started by Frank Li at 2026-03-18T11:05:02Z.
perf-bot: Canary metrics: initial 5m look — p95 improved by 45% vs canary before patch. KV hit-rate unchanged immediately.
Eva Singh: After 10m, KV hit-rate creeping back up ~6%. Suggest monitoring eviction rate; if it steadies maybe batching coalescing recovers.
Ben Carter: If affinity tweak holds, we should follow with a slow increase of mini-window shrink in 2 steps instead of 16->8 jump.
Carla Mendez: Agreed. I'll open a short RFC for rollout plan and add a regression test that simulates high small-seq QPS to catch this in CI.
Alice Nguyen: Great. For now let's keep the canary running and hold full rollout. Thanks everyone :thumbsup:

title: #incidents: 1934567890
preview:
Alex Kim: FYI SFO GPUs saw a sustained eviction spike at 02:12 UTC, dropping available capacity ~18% for ~14m. Triggered weighted shift but some clients hit tail latency spikes.

Maya Patel: Thanks. Do we know root cause? evictions from host churn or node OOMs?

Alex Kim: Early signs point to simultaneous node reboots after a kernel patch + aggressive kube eviction threshold (we changed default from 0.5 -> 0.7 last week). Node churn + large batch jobs failed to drain cleanly.

Juan Ortega: We saw burst autoscale try to allocate, but reserve pool ran dry. Request routing fell back to cross-region which added 30-40ms p99.

Sophie Li: Key ask: update runbook to smooth eviction reaction and add cooldown before shifting full weight. Current playbook immediate 100% shift is too aggressive.

Ben Carter: Proposed changes:
1) Add 10m cooldown after first eviction surge before 100% shift.
2) Progressive weights: 25% -> 50% -> 75% -> 100% over 5m steps if eviction persists.
3) Increase reserve pool target from 10% -> 15% for SFO during peak hours.
4) Add automatic billing tag when burst capacity > 30% to flag cost impact.

Priya Rao: Also add observability hooks: per-node eviction rate metric, per-pod preemption counter, and a dashboard panel with "reserve_remaining" + "burst_cost_estimate".

Alex Kim: I'll PR playbook edits. Draft snippet for traffic shift helper:
```
# progressive shift helper (pseudo)
for w in [25,50,75,100]:
  curl -XPOST https://routing.redwood/api/shift -d '{"region":"sfo","weight":'+str(w)+'}'
  sleep 60
```

ops-bot: Incident created: INC-2026-1934567890, severity=high, timeline attached.

Maya Patel: Action items:
- Alex -> update runbook + add cooldown logic (due EOD UTC)
- Priya -> dashboard panels + alert (due +48h)
- Ben -> reserve pool sizing proposal + cost estimate (due +72h)
- Juan -> add unit tests for progressive shift helper (due +72h)

Sophie Li: Add an explicit rollback step to return weights back to baseline if latency improves; add a canary check to validate p99 under 150ms for 5m before increasing to next weight.

Ben Carter: Noted. Will include canary gating in proposal.

Alex Kim: Quick followups: need a short KB blurb so support can explain the burst cost tag to customers. I'll draft.

Juan Ortega: Temporary mitigation: we've enabled a 12m warm pool pre-provision during peak window for SFO, lowered batch job concurrency where possible.

Priya Rao: I added the alert template: "SFO eviction_rate > 0.2 for 3m" -> runbook link. Will push to staging.

Sophie Li: Thanks all. Let's reconvene after PRs land. :thumbsup: 

Ben Carter: ack. (reaction :white_check_mark:)

incident-bot: Incident INC-2026-1934567890 status updated -> monitoring.

title: #eng-infra: 1923456789
preview:
Alice Nguyen: Heads-up — we just hit an onboarding blocker for a new customer in eastus2. Opened an Azure case but want to coordinate escalation. Case created: 210123456789.
Ben Carter: Case #? subscription? Also are these dedicated reserved nodes or spot?
Alice Nguyen: case 210123456789 (linked in portal). Subscription ID: xxxxx-aaaa-1111. We need reserved-pref capacity but can accept spot for the initial burst. Main constraint is latency for customers in eastus2, so prefer same region.
Priya Rao: Cost note — NVv4 reserved will be cheaper long-term but we should validate perf vs A100-ish SKUs first. If azure can't supply NVv4 at scale in eastus2, consider euapac fallback or fallback to A100 in nearby region.
Diego Morales: Adding: customer needs private connectivity to their VNet (ExpressRoute) — will need enough public IPs and NAT ports for 8 nodes. Can we ask Azure to provision an isolated quota pool?
Azure Support Bot: New support update for case 210123456789: Initial triage complete. Assigned to engineer. ETA for quota review: 48-72 hours. Reference: azure.support/portal/cases/210123456789
Alice Nguyen: Thanks. If we can push this, ask them whether NVv4 allocation can be reserved for our subscription or if it's per-tenant pool. We had a similar limit where platform-wide availability blocked us.
Maya (Azure CSE): Hi team — I'm Maya, I'll be your CSE. Looking at the case: eastus2 NVv4 capacity is constrained this week. We can submit a capacity request but need: (1) exact VM sku list, (2) expected start/end dates, (3) business justification, (4) VNet/subnet IDs for attachment. Also please confirm if accelerated networking required on boot or can be enabled post-provisioning.
Ben Carter: VM SKUs: Standard_NV8as_v4 x2 initially, ramp to x8 over 14 days. Start date target: 2026-04-01. Business justification: production inference serving for Redwood customer with SLA requirement. Accelerated networking required at boot to pass perf tests.
Alice Nguyen: Provided vNet/subnet in portal. Justification added to case comments. Also asked our AM to loop in platform contacts — putting @Diego in case thread.
Maya (Azure CSE): Thanks — updated case 210123456789. I can elevate to Capacity Team if you mark as "Urgent" and provide a short runbook for failover/alternate region. Otherwise standard quota path ~72 hours. Note: we can offer a temporary "burst" allocation for 48 hours while capacity is arranged, but we need approval from Capacity Team.
Priya Rao: Temporary burst would be perfect to run validation and switch traffic gradually. Diego can you draft the failover plan?

Priya Rao: westus2 has better NVv4 availability historically — but check data residency. If customers require eastus2 only, we'll need the burst plan + temporary fallback to A100 in eu-west to keep latency acceptable.
Alice Nguyen: Noted. For tracking: keeping this thread as the coordination source. I'll post the Azure case updates here as they arrive. :eyes:
Maya (Azure CSE): Quick follow-up — I need the exact subscription contact email (for approval) and a copy of the purchase order or contract line referencing reserved capacity. Please upload to the case so Capacity Team can approve faster.
Diego Morales: Uploading contract snippet now.
Alice Nguyen: Uploaded PO. Marking request as urgent in case comments.

title: #customer-success: 1918750001
preview:
Aisha Khan: FYI customer ACME just escalated — after the v3.2-quant rollout their invoice-parsing accuracy dropped ~30% for a specific prompt set. Internal evals (same test suite) show no regression. Can we triage? :eyes:
Ben Carter: assigning to eng-ml + runtime. Do we have the customer prompt and an example failure?
Aisha Khan: pasted below (sanitized). They say it used to return a structured JSON with fields, now sometimes returns free text or misses the 'invoice_total' field entirely. Customer prompt:
```
Please extract invoice fields: vendor, invoice_number, invoice_date, invoice_total
Invoice text: "ACME Corp\u00A01001
Date: 2025-11-05
Total: $4,320.00"
```
Note: the space between 'ACME Corp' and '1001' appears to be a non-breaking space (\u00A0) in the original customer payload.
Priya Nair: interesting — internal tests probably used normal spaces. Tokenization/normalization differences could explain mismatch. Did rollout change tokenizer or quantization that affects token boundaries?
Evan Li: runtime didn't change tokenizer intentionally. v3.2-quant included new fused attention kernel and a different packing path. Quantization can change decoding slightly. But 30% is big. Do we have the exact API input/response from the customer?
Aisha Khan: customer sent their raw request (sanitized):
```json
{"model":"redwood-v3-quant","input":"Please extract invoice fields: vendor, invoice_number, invoice_date, invoice_total
Invoice text: \"ACME Corp\u00A01001
Date: 2025-11-05
Total: $4,320.00\"","temperature":0.0}
```
Customer observed response:
```json
{"vendor":"ACME Corp1001","invoice_number":"","invoice_date":"2025-11-05","invoice_total":"4320.00"}
```
Expected (pre-rollout):
```json
{"vendor":"ACME Corp","invoice_number":"1001","invoice_date":"2025-11-05","invoice_total":"4320.00"}
```
support-bot: Created ticket #CS-4321 and linked ACME's logs.
Ben Carter: can we reproduce locally by sending the exact payload (with NBSP) to the canary and to previous model variant? Priya, can you try tokenizing that string in our tokenizer to see token boundaries?
Priya Nair: running tokenizer now. Quick result: when the NBSP is present, tokenizer produces a single token spanning "Corp\u00A01001" for the v3 tokenizer config. With normal space it splits into two tokens. That would explain vendor+invoice_number concatenation at generation with deterministic decoding.
Evan Li: good find. So internal evals likely normalized whitespace before tests. ACME's prompt preserved NBSP. Two paths: (1) update our normalization on ingest to normalize NBSPs, (2) add a check in rollout to run customer prompt sets unchanged. Which is safer short-term?
Aisha Khan: customer asks for quick mitigation — they prefer a patch on our side so they don't need to change their pipeline. Can we enable a preprocessing normalization for text inputs for hosted API only?
Ben Carter: engineering wise, adding a sanitizer that normalizes NBSP -> regular space before tokenization is low-risk. But need to audit for potential side effects (languages where NBSP matters). We can gate it behind a feature flag for the hosted API.
Priya Nair: I'll prototype a normalized path and run the customer's prompt set across canary vs prod. Also will re-run internal evals with NBSP variants to estimate regression sensitivity.
Evan Li: +1. Also check the quantized model's tokenization behavior doesn't differ across builds — upstream tokenizer weights/config mismatch is possible. Log the tokenizer config used in the model artifact.
Aisha Khan: communicating to customer: "we're investigating; temporary mitigation available (flag + canary) — we'll follow up in <4h with plan." They confirmed that's acceptable.
Ben Carter: action items: Priya prototype sanitizer + tests, Evan validate model tokenizer config and artifact consistency, QA run customer's prompt set with NBSP and normal space on v3.1 and v3.2-quant, Support update CS-4321 with ETA.
Priya Nair: started local repro: curl to canary shows the same concatenation. Example curl:
```bash
curl -X POST https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer REDACTED" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"model":"redwood-v3-quant","input":"Please extract invoice fields: vendor, invoice_number, invoice_date, invoice_total
Invoice text: \"ACME Corp\u00A01001
Date: 2025-11-05
Total: $4,320.00\"","temperature":0.0}'
```
Response reproduces customer case. Running tokenizer patch now.
Evan Li: if sanitizer fixes it, we should rollout to hosted quickly and then schedule a model-artifact audit for Dedicated/Private customers with a note about tokenizer parity.
Aisha Khan: customer wants notes for their compliance team. Can we include a short explanation of the root cause and patch plan in CS-4321?
Ben Carter: yes, include: root cause (NBSP tokenized as part of token -> concatenation), short term fix (input normalization on hosted API), medium term (artifact/tokenizer parity checks + eval harness include raw customer prompt sets), long term (add whitespace-varied tests in release gating).
Priya Nair: patch works in local canary — vendor and invoice_number separate now. I'll open PR and request urgent review.
support-bot: PR #8765 opened by priya.nair (label: urgent, changelog: input-normalization).
Evan Li: great. Once PR merges, let's run the customer's full prompt set, then canary deploy and notify ACME after verification.
Aisha Khan: thanks everyone. I'll keep CS-4321 updated and share reproduction steps with ACME for transparency. :handshake:

title: #customer-success: 1901234567
preview:
Maria Gomez: Quick weekly check-in for Quasar Health — they had 3 repeat 503s Friday afternoon (approx 15:02 UTC) during a billing spike. I logged the customer ticket (CS-8742). Can we confirm owners and next steps?

Ben Carter: I saw that. Root cause looks like connection pool exhaustion on the dedicated gateway. Error sample: `ERR_HTTP2_STREAM_CANCEL` in the logs.

Tim Liu: Assigned infra action. I'll take ownership of the pool tuning and short-term mitigations. Planning: bump pool size + add stricter circuit-breaker.

Aisha Patel: Thanks @Tim. I'll own the customer comms and update CS-8742 with the mitigation ETA. Also flagging renewal risk if this repeats.

Ben Carter: Short-term patch pushed to staging now. Deploy Bot: canary results?

Deploy Bot: Canary deploy to quasar-canary-2 completed at 2029-11-02T16:18:30Z — 0 errors in 20m smoke tests. :white_check_mark:

Tim Liu: Rolling the config to prod tonight at 22:00 UTC with a 10-minute canary. Action items: (1) increase pool from 200 -> 400, (2) add connection backoff on 5xx, (3) enable prefix KV cache for billing route.

Ben Carter: Adding a retry-after header handler on the client side to reduce retry storms. I'll open infra ticket INF-2198 and link to CS-8742.

Aisha Patel: I'll schedule a 15-min sync with the Quasar PM on Monday to walk through mitigations and offer an enablement doc for their engineers (best practices for retries + idempotency).

Maria Gomez: Ownership summary for the thread: Tim = infra/migration, Ben = client-side mitigations + INF-2198, Aisha = customer comms + enablement, Maria = escalation owner and renewal flag. Please confirm and drop any blockers.

Tim Liu: Confirmed. No blockers from infra, just need escalation window tonight (22:00-22:30 UTC).

Ben Carter: Confirmed. Will have INF-2198 link by EOD.

Aisha Patel: Confirmed. I'll send the calendar invite for Mon 10:00am PT and update CS-8742 with the interim workaround.

Maria Gomez: Excellent. Follow-ups: Aisha to post customer-facing notes, Tim to update post-deploy metrics in #eng-monitoring, Ben to attach client patch. We'll reconvene if repeat errors show up. :rocket:

preview:
Anna Liu: Hey finance team — BoxedCo's March invoice rendered incorrectly in tonight's batch. The subscription line is missing and there's a phantom promo credit of -$1,200. PDF preview came back blank. Can someone take a look?
Raj Patel: Can you paste the invoice id and whether this was just for BoxedCo or larger batch?
Anna Liu: invoice id INV-2026-00789, single-customer run inside the nightly batch (customer-specific billing). Here's what the generation output gave us: 
```
{"id":"INV-2026-00789","customer":"BoxedCo","lines":[{"desc":"promo credit","amount_cents":-120000},{"desc":"tax","amount_cents":24000}],"total_cents":-96000}
```
Raj Patel: ugh — so subscription line (expected ~1,200,000 cents) is absent. Any render errors in logs?
Billing Bot: billing-renderer-2: failed to render PDF for INV-2026-00789 at 2026-03-23T02:14:07Z — error=\"missing template data: subscription_lines\" job=br-20260323-77
Maria Gomez: Likely the promo-sync job wrote a negative line before subscription aggregation completed, renderer saw partial invoice object and proceeded. Could be race between promotion reconciliation and invoice assembly. Please don't reissue yet if the invoice was already emailed.
Anna Liu: Not emailed yet. Customer preview not sent. We can regenerate.
Ben Carter: Short-term fix: re-run invoice generation for that invoice after pausing promo-sync. Command: `billing-cli invoice regenerate --id INV-2026-00789 --force` (this will update ledger). Need explicit approval from finance lead to mutate ledger.
Anna Liu: Approve re-run, go ahead. :thumbsup:
Raj Patel: Running now — pausing promo-sync, issuing regenerate...
Raj Patel: Result: regenerate returned success. New generated payload: 
```
{"id":"INV-2026-00789","customer":"BoxedCo","lines":[{"desc":"subscription (Mar)","amount_cents":1200000},{"desc":"tax","amount_cents":240000}],"total_cents":1440000}
```
Raj Patel: PDF preview rendered OK and attached to thread (preview link: https://files.redwood/invoices/INV-2026-00789/preview.pdf). Promo credit is no longer present.
Maria Gomez: Thanks. Please file a follow-up ticket to root-cause: possible race between promo-sync and invoice assembly + renderer not validating required fields. Assign to eng-billing and set P1 for next sprint planning.
Ben Carter: Filed ENG-5342: "Invoice assembly race causes missing lines / invalid render" and added steps to reproduce + logs. Assigning to @eng-billing.
Anna Liu: Great. I'll notify support to hold outreach to BoxedCo until we've confirmed final invoice.
Raj Patel: Closing thread for now — will re-open if anything else surfaces.

title: #docs: 1851234567
preview:
Nora Kim: opened PR #611 to standardize rate limit headers and add a small migration note in the billing + dashboards page. PR includes a new example panel and a tiny troubleshooting section. https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/611
Rafael Gomez: nice. which header names are we settling on? we have `X-RW-RateLimit-Limit` floating around in a few internal snippets.
Nora Kim: agreed on `X-RW-Ratelimit-Limit`, `X-RW-Ratelimit-Remaining`, and `Retry-After` for 429s. We're lowercasing the internal examples to match our SDK header access helpers (some languages use case-insensitive lookup). Old `X-RateLimit-*` headers will be supported for 14 days.
Priya Singh: can we add a short curl example showing the backoff guidance? might help customers handle 429s when they migrate.
Nora Kim: good call. Draft curl and Go snippets below — Rafael can you sanity-check the Go one?
Priya Singh: curl snippet: ```bash
curl -i -H "Authorization: Bearer $RW_KEY" https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate -d '{"prompt":"hi"}'
# look for headers: Retry-After, X-RW-Ratelimit-Remaining
```
Priya Singh: Go snippet: ```go
resp, _ := client.Generate(ctx, req)
limit := resp.Header.Get("X-RW-Ratelimit-Limit")
remaining := resp.Header.Get("X-RW-Ratelimit-Remaining")
retry := resp.Header.Get("Retry-After")
if resp.StatusCode == http.StatusTooManyRequests {
    waitSec, _ := strconv.Atoi(retry)
    time.Sleep(time.Duration(waitSec) * time.Second)
}
log.Printf("quota %s/%s", remaining, limit)
```
Rafael Gomez: Go looks good; minor nit: use `strings.TrimSpace` on header values before Atoi to be safe. I'll push that tiny fix to the PR.
Ben Carter: on the dashboard side  the panel we include in docs referenced a variable `$org` but our current dashboards use `$team`. I updated the exported panel JSON to use `$team` and put it under `/assets/grafana/panels/quota_panel_team.json`. Also added a caption about the metric name `rw_quota_consumed_total`.
Nora Kim: thanks Ben. Can you also add a short note in the pricing tiers page explaining how headers map to token quotas? Product wants one clear sentence and a link to the Grafana panel.
Ben Carter: added the sentence and linked the panel asset. I also updated the screenshot to show the updated legend label `Quota (rw_quota_consumed_total)` so it matches the caption.
Rafael Gomez: small accessibility note: the screenshot had very small axis labels. I replaced it with the high-resolution export and compressed the file. PR now includes the new image under `/assets/images/quota-panel-hires.png`.
Priya Singh: migration guide: added a 'quick diff' showing old -> new header names and a troubleshooting bullet: "If your reverse proxy strips `Retry-After`, ensure passthrough of 4xx headers (nginx `proxy_pass_request_headers on;`)."
Nora Kim: that troubleshooting tip is perfect. Also added a short blurb about SDKs: we recommend using the SDK header helper functions where available instead of raw header parsing.
DocsBot: PR #611 status changed: reviewers requested @rafael, @ben, @priya.
Rafael Gomez: approved after the TrimSpace change.
Ben Carter: approved (image preview looks good).
Priya Singh: approved.
DocsBot: PR #611 merged by Nora Kim.
Nora Kim: merged. I'll follow up with a small announcement in #docs and a note in the internal migration tracker. Thanks all! :sparkles:

title: #all-hands: 1842509999
preview:
Noah Kim: Quick Q from the all-hands: can we get a concise definition of patch vs minor vs major for the release calendar?
Zara Hughes: Yup. Short answer: patch = urgent bug/security fix; minor = regular feature/iterative work; major = API or infra contract change or anything that needs cross-team migration.
Maya Singh: Also need clarity on frequency: you mentioned 'cadence' in the talk — is that frozen or guidance?
Ava Chen (CTO): Guidance. Plan is: weekly small patches as needed; a two-week minor cadence (feature train); and a monthly window for bigger platform/major-compat releases. Exceptions allowed with RFC and coordinating owners.
Ben Carter (EngOps): For rollouts we'll use staged expansion with monitoring gates. Suggested default: 1% -> 10% -> 50% -> 100%. Smaller services can skip steps.
Noah Kim: What monitoring gates should be in the PR?
Zara Hughes: Include: baseline error rate, baseline p95 latency, a list of synthetic "golden" prompts for perf and correctness checks, and any route-specific counters. Ben will add a small checklist to the PR template. :white_check_mark:
Oncall-bot: 
```
Default rollout timeline:
- 1% (5m checks)
- 10% (15m checks)
- 50% (30m checks)
- 100% (1h stabilization)
Auto-cut triggers (any one):
- 5xx rate increase > 0.2% absolute for 10m
- request error rate > 3x baseline for 5m
- p95 latency > 1.5x baseline for 15m
- increase in model hallucination signals (eval drift) > threshold
```
Maya Singh: I like the 1% start for risky changes. How involved is oncall at each step?
Ben Carter (EngOps): Oncall watches for alerts and has authority to pause/rollback. They do not need to manually ack each expansion unless an alert fires. Owners must post progress in #release-updates.
Noah Kim: What are the quality gates pre-merge for a minor?
Zara Hughes: Required: passing unit tests, staging smoke, perf regression test against the golden prompt set (no >4% regression in throughput or p95), and model-output regression check for any generation/model-selection changes. E2E tests when routing/model selector is affected.
Ava Chen (CTO): Important: any change affecting model selection, quantization, or tokenization needs a model-eval run and a second reviewer from ML infra.
Maya Singh: Post-release: when do we expect incident writeups?
Zara Hughes: Any customer-impacting regression or rollback => 72h mini postmortem filled in the playbook. Less severe issues should still get a short incident note within a week.
Noah Kim: SLA tie-ins: does the cadence change when error budget is low?
Ava Chen (CTO): Yes. If monthly error budget remaining < 15% we auto-enforce a freeze for non-critical changes until engineering triage clears the budget. EngOps publishes alerts at 50% and again at 15%.
Ben Carter (EngOps): Enforcement: automatic block in release tool when <15% error budget. Exceptions need exec signoff documented in the release ticket.
Maya Singh: Branching policy for hotfixes?
Zara Hughes: Hotfixes: branch from stable (patch/*), include changelog and tests, and get two approvers if touching public APIs. Merge back to main after validation.
Noah Kim: Can Ben drop the PR checklist and a short cheatsheet into #eng-process?
Ben Carter (EngOps): Will do. I'll post a one-pager with the default rollout snippet and PR checklist by EOD tomorrow.
Ava Chen (CTO): Thanks — we'll add the one-pager to the onboarding playbook and link it from the release RFC template.
Noah Kim: Appreciated, this clears up a lot.

preview:
Nora Finch: opened a tiny PR for the 0.10.2 changelog. Main items: function-calling examples updated, one docs-only fix for link rot, and a note about a small regression in structured output formatting. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/docs/pull/4177

Alex Morgan: thanks. what's the structured output regression? severity?

Nora Finch: when using function/tool calling with streaming + JSON schema enforcement, nested arrays could be emitted with trailing commas causing strict parsers to fail. Low volume but bad for strict clients.

Priya Singh: ugh. we should give a short workaround and point to the SDK snippet showing tolerant parsing + recommended schema.

Nora Finch: suggested changelog blurb: "Known issue: some structured outputs emitted during streaming may include trailing commas, which can break strict JSON parsers. Workaround: disable streaming for function calls or use tolerant JSON parsing. Fix in next patch."

Ben Carter: please add a small JS snippet for tolerant parsing and the explicit non-streaming call. I can paste into the PR.

Priya Singh: snippet idea: show using the SDK plus JSON5 parsing fallback. Small two-line example so copy/paste works. Also add link to /guides/parsing/structured-output.md

Nora Finch: I'll add both: short snippet + link. Also noted a link-fix: old reference to /examples/old-func-call should instead go to /examples/function-calls/. Jen asked us to sweep those links — assigning to Docs Bot to remind.

Docs Bot: Reminder created: sweep links in changelog and examples. Assigned to Jen H.

Alex Morgan: add QA step in PR: run structured-output test suite with strict JSON parser enabled and streaming toggled off/on. That will catch regressions.

Ben Carter: added that test note to the PR description.

Priya Singh: wording tweak: shorten changelog line to "Temporary: streaming function outputs may include trailing commas; disable streaming or use tolerant parsing." Fits one-liner.

Nora Finch: good edit. tagging @Alex Morgan @Priya Singh for approvals.

Alex Morgan: approving.

Priya Singh: approving. Merge when CI passes. :shipit:
